Learning Focus:
Learning Focus:
Chapter 15 _ Aggregate Demand & Aggregate Supply
Type 1 (Change in ADAS)
Example Question 1 DSEPPMCQ23
Refer to the following diagram. 參考下圖。
Which of the following will cause a movement along the aggregate demand curve from point A to point B?
A. a drop in price levelB. a decrease in the interest rate
C. a reduction in salaries taxD. a depreciation of domestic currency
下列哪項會導致沿著總需求曲線從A點移至B點？
A. 物價水平下降B. 利率下降
C. 減低薪俸稅D. 本地貨幣貶值
Example Question 2 DSEPPMCQ24
A large number of oil fields have to be closed because of serious oil spill accidents. In the short run, the aggregate supply of an oil-importing country will __________ and its price level will ________.
A. increase … rise B. increase … fall C. decrease … rise D. decrease … fall
大量油田因嚴重漏油意外需要關閉。短期而言，一石油輸出國的總供應會_____，而該國的物價水平會_______。
增加 … 上升        B. 增加 … 下降        C. 減少 … 上升        D. 減少 … 下降
Example Question 3 DSE2012MCQ25
Which of the following will lead to a decrease in aggregate demand?
A. a rise in private savingB. a r

ggregate demand?
A. a rise in private savingB. a rise in investment expenditure
C. a fall in importsD. a fall in the progressivity of income tax
下列哪項會令總需求下降？
A. 私人儲蓄增加                  B. 投資支出增加
C. 進口減少                      D. 入息稅的累進程度減低
Example Question 4 DSE2012MCQ34
Suppose the government spends $3 billion on the construction of a cruise terminal which is financed from its reserves. This will result in
(1) an increase in aggregate output in the short run.
(2) an increase in aggregate output in the long run.
(3) an increase in employment in the short run.
(4) a budget deficit in the years of construction.
A. (1) and (3) onlyB. (2) and (4) onlyC. (1), (2) and (3) onlyD. (1), (3) and (4) only
假設政府耗資30億元興建一郵輪碼頭，該筆開支由政府的儲備支付。這會導致下列哪些結果？
(1) 短期而言，總產出上升。
(2) 長期而言，總產出上升。
(3) 短期而言，就業增加。
(4) 在建築年期內出現財政赤字。
只有(1)、(3)        B. 只有(2)、(4)       C. 只有(1)、(2)、(3)        D. 只有(1)、(3)、(4)
Example Question 5 DSE2012MCQ36
Which of the following can offset the effect of a decrease in income tax rate on aggregate demand?
A. A d

ase in income tax rate on aggregate demand?
A. A decrease in the asset demand for moneyB. A decrease in the desire to import
C. An increase in the desire to investD. An increase in the required reserve ratio
下列哪項能抵銷入息稅稅率下降對總需求的影響？
A. 資產性貨幣需求下降B. 進口意欲下降
C. 投資意欲上升D. 法定儲備比率上升
Example Question 6 DSE2013MCQ29
Suppose the government greatly increases the tax allowance on continuing education. Which of the following can best explain the effect of the above change on the price and output levels?
假設政府大幅增加持續教育的免稅額。下列哪幅圖最能解釋上述改變對物價水平和產出水平的影響？
***Example Question 7 DSE2014MCQ27
Refer to the above diagram. Which of the following would most likely lead to the shift of the short run aggregate supply curve from SRAS0 to SRAS1?
A. To relax the quota for foreign domestic helpersB. To reduce the subsidy in research and development
C. To increase the profits tax rateD. To increase the number of places in tertiary education
參閱上圖。下列哪項最有可能導致短期總供應曲線由SRAS0移至SRAS1？
A. 放寬外籍家庭傭工的配額B. 減少在研究開發方面的補貼
C. 增加利得稅率D. 增加大專教育的學位數量
Example Question

研究開發方面的補貼
C. 增加利得稅率D. 增加大專教育的學位數量
Example Question 8 DSE2014MCQ28
The Old Age Living Allowance (長者生活津貼) introduced by the Hong Kong government increases the ________________ in GDP and results in a _________ shift of the aggregate demand curve.
A. government consumption expenditure … rightwardB. government consumption expenditure … leftward
C. private consumption expenditure … rightwardD. private consumption expenditure … leftward
香港政府增設長者生活津貼令本地生產總值內的_______________增加，並引致總需求曲線向_______移。
A. 政府消費支出 … 右B. 政府消費支出 … 左
C. 私人消費支出 … 右D. 私人消費支出 … 左
Example Question 9 DSE2014MCQ29
If there is a decrease in national income of a major trading partner of an economy, the employment of the economy will _____ and the price level will _______ in the short run.
A. increase ... increaseB. increase … decrease
C. decrease … increaseD. decrease ... decrease
若某經濟一個主要貿易夥伴的國民收入下跌，在短期該經濟的就業會_______而物價水平會_________。
A. 增加 … 上升                          B. 增加 … 下跌
C. 減少 … 上升                         D. 減少 … 下跌
Example Question 10 DSE2014M

D. 減少 … 下跌
Example Question 10 DSE2014MCQ35
Which of the following policies can the government introduce to reduce the price and unemployment levels at the same time?
A. To raise the discount ratesB. To raise the subsidies to small and medium-sized enterprises
C. To raise the salaries tax rateD. To raise the expenditure on social welfare
政府可以採用下列哪項政策同時調低物價和失業水平？
A. 提高貼現率               B. 增加對中小企業的補貼
C. 提高薪俸稅率             D. 增加社會福利支出
Example Question 11 DSE2014MCQ38
Which of the following government policies will lead to an increase in the aggregate output of a country in the long run?
(1) To tighten regulations environment protection
(2) To grant an amnesty (特赦) to illegal immigrants
(3) To ban the importation of foreign domestic helpers
(4) To build more international schools for attracting expatriates to work in the country
A. (1) and (3) onlyB. (1) and (4) onlyC. (2) and (3) onlyD. (2) and (4) only
長期而言，下列哪些政府政策會增加某國的總產出？
(1) 收緊環境保護的法例
(2) 特赦非法入境者
(3) 禁止輸入外籍家庭傭工
(4) 興建更多國際學校以吸引外國人到該國工作
A. 只有(1)、(

(3) 禁止輸入外籍家庭傭工
(4) 興建更多國際學校以吸引外國人到該國工作
A. 只有(1)、(3)          B. 只有(1)、(4)         C. 只有(2)、(3)        D. 只有(2)、(4)
Example Question 12 DSE2015MCQ26
Which of the following will result in a rightward shift of the aggregate demand curve of a country?
A. The central bank reduces the discount rate.B. The minimum wage rate is increased.
C. The general price level decreases.D. Its trading partner suffers from an economic recession.
下列哪項會引致某國的總需求曲線向右移？
A. 中央銀行調低貼現率B. 最低工資率提高了
C. 一般物價水平下降      D. 其貿易夥伴遭逢經濟衰退
Example Question 13 DSE2015MCQ27
The government provides cash subsidies for new-born children from their birth to age 10. Which of the following diagrams best illustrates the effect of the subsidies on the economy after twenty years?
政府向剛出生的小孩發放現金補貼，直至他們年滿10歲。下列哪幅圖最能說明該補貼對該經濟二十年後的影響？
Example Question 14 DSE2015MCQ37
Refer to the following aggregate supply diagram. 細閱下列的總供應圖。
When the economy moves from point A to point B, which of the following will NOT change?
(1) Real output
(2) Nominal output
(3) Potential out

) Real output
(2) Nominal output
(3) Potential output
A. (1) onlyB. (2) onlyC. (1) and (3) onlyD. (1), (2) and (3)
當經濟由A點移至B點，下列哪(幾)項不會改變？
(1) 實質產出
(2) 名義產出
(3) 潛在產出
A. 只有(1)     B. 只有(2) C. 只有(1)、(3)  D. (1)、(2)及(3)
Example Question 15 DSE2015MCQ39
In an economy with severe inflation and income inequality, the government can lower the price level and reduce the income gap by
A. Increasing the environmental tax on handling household sewage.
B. Increasing the salaries tax and its progressivity.
C. Reducing its spending on social welfare.
D. Reducing the personal allowance of the salaries tax.
某經濟出現嚴重通脹和收入不均，政府可以籍________以減低物價水平和收窄收入差距
A. 增加處理住戶污水的環境稅。B. 提高薪俸稅及其累進程度。
C. 減少它在社會福利的開支。D. 減低薪俸稅的個人免稅額。
Example Question 16 DSE2016MCQ26
The aggregate demand of a country will drop if
A. The price level of the country rises.B. The currency of its major trading partner appreciates.
C. The government spending on social welfare rises.D. The required reserve ratio rises.
在下列哪種情況下，某國的總需求會下降？
A. 該國的物價水平上升。                   B

哪種情況下，某國的總需求會下降？
A. 該國的物價水平上升。                   B. 該國的主要貿易夥伴的貨幣升值。
C. 政府的社會福利開支上升。               D. 法定儲備比率上升。
 
Example Question 17 DSE2016MCQ27
The government builds new infrastructures, there will be an increase in
(1) employed population during the construction period.
(2) the price level during the construction period.
(3) potential output after the construction is completed.
(4) the price level after the construction is completed.
A. (1) and (2) onlyB. (3) and (4) onlyC. (1), (2) and (3) onlyD. (1), (2), (3) and (4)
若政府興建新的基建，這會令___________上升。
(1) 建築期間的就業人口
(2) 建築期間的物價水平
(3) 基建完成後的潛在產出
(4) 基建完成後的物價水平
A. 只有(1)、(2)         B. 只有(3)、(4)        C. 只有(1)、(2)、(3)       D. (1)、(2)、(3)及(4)
Example Question 18 DSE2016MCQ28
The ‘multiple entry permit’ (一簽多行) for the permanent Shenzhen residents to visit Hong Kong has been replaced by a ‘one trip per week cap’(一週一行). This would reduce Hong Kong’s _____ and _____.
A. Aggregate demand ... output level in the long runB. Aggregate demand ... price level in the long ru

B. Aggregate demand ... price level in the long run
C. Short-run aggregate supply ... output level in the short runD. Long-run aggregate supply ... output level in the long run
深圳永久居民往香港的「一簽多行」被「一週一行」所取代。這會令香港的_____和_____下跌。
A. 總需求 … 長期產出水平B. 總需求 … 長期物價水平
C. 短期總供應 … 短期產出水平D. 長期總供應 … 長期產出水平
***Example Question 19 DSE2016MCQ38
If the government simultaneously increases its expenditure and income tax by the same amount, the effect on the aggregate output is _____________.
A. contractionaryB. neutralC. expansionaryD. indeterminate
假設政府同時增加支出和入息稅，兩者增加的金額相同。這對總產出的影響屬
A. 收縮性           B. 中性      C. 擴張性          D. 未能確定
Example Question 20 DSE2016MCQ40
Under which of the following situations may a fiscal deficit and a trade deficit be eliminated at the same time?
(1) a decrease in nominal interest rate
(2) a depreciation of the economy’s currency
(3) an increase in income tax
A. (1) and (2) onlyB. (1) and (3) onlyC. (2) and (3) onlyD. (1), (2) and (3)
在下列哪些情況下，財政赤字和貿易逆差可以同時被消除？
(1) 名義利率下降
(2) 該經濟的貨幣貶值
(3) 入息稅上升
A. 只有

逆差可以同時被消除？
(1) 名義利率下降
(2) 該經濟的貨幣貶值
(3) 入息稅上升
A. 只有(1)、(2)        B. 只有(1)、(3)  C. 只有(2)、(3)      D. (1)、(2)及(3)
Example Question 21 DSE2017MCQ28
The aggregate demand curve of Hong Kong will shift to the left if
A. the implicit GDP deflator rises continuously B. the government raises the retirement age of all civil servants
C. the number of tourists from the Mainland decreasesD. the Mainland stock market booms
若______________________，香港的總需求曲線會向左移
A. 本地生產總值內含平減指數持續上升B. 政府提高全體公務員的退休年齡
C. 內地到港旅客人數下降D. 內地股市興旺
Example Question 22 DSE2017MCQ29
The diagram above shows the aggregate demand curves and long run aggregate supply curves of an economy. Which of the following best explains the above changes?
A. A decrease in people’s desire to save
B. An enhancement in production technology
C. An increase in national income of the economy’s trading partners
D. An increase in government subsidy on private research and development
上圖顯示某經濟的總需求曲線和長期總供應曲線。下列哪項最能解釋以上轉變？
A. 人們的儲蓄意欲下降B. 生產技術提升
C. 該經濟的貿易夥伴的國民收入上升D. 政府對私人研究及發展對補貼增加
E

下降B. 生產技術提升
C. 該經濟的貿易夥伴的國民收入上升D. 政府對私人研究及發展對補貼增加
Example Question 23 DSE2017MCQ40
In his 2016-17 Budget, the Financial Secretary proposed to waive business registration fees from 2016-17. At the same time, the personal basic tax allowance was raised from $120,000 to $132,000. Which of the following best illustrates the effect of the above changes on the price and output levels?
財政司司長在2016-17預算案內建議豁免2016-17年度的商業登記費，同時將個人基本免稅額由$120 000增加至
$132 000。下列哪圖最能說明上述轉變對物價和產出水平的影響？
Example Question 24 DSE2018MCQ26
The short run aggregate supply curve will shift to the right if
A. the government relaxes the importation quota of foreign workersB. the government increases the profit tax rate
C. the central bank sells government bonds to the publicD. there is an increase in the price level 
下列哪種情況下，短期總供應曲線會向右移？
A. 政府放寬輸入外國工人的配額B. 政府提高利得稅率
C. 中央銀行向公眾出售政府債券D. 物價水平上升
Example Question 25 DSE2018MCQ27
Which of the following will lead to an increase in both price level and aggregate output in the short run?
A. There is an improvem

e output in the short run?
A. There is an improvement in production technologyB. There is an increase in the oil price
C. There is a decrease in money supply D. There is a decrease in the desire to save
下列哪項引致物價水平和總產出在短期內均會上升？
A. 生產技術提升B. 油價上升
C. 貨幣供應下降D. 人們儲蓄意慾下降
Example Question 26 DSE2018MCQ38
Suppose there is a reduction in the basic allowance of the salaries tax in an economy. Which of the following can offset its effect on aggregate demand?
A. People’s desire to save increases
B. The price level decreases
C. The central bank carries out an open market sale of government bonds
D. The required reserve ratio in the banking system decreases
假設某經濟中薪俸稅的基本免稅額下降了。下列哪項可以抵銷其對總需求的影響？
A. 人們的儲蓄意欲增加B. 物價水平下降
C. 中央銀行於公開市場出售政府債券D. 銀行體系的法定儲備比率下降
Example Question 27 DSE2019MCQ27
Country A is an oil-importing country. Which of the following will result in a rightward shift of its short-run aggregate supply curve?
A. All oil-exporting countries decide to increase the daily production of oil
B. Extreme cold weather sharply

production of oil
B. Extreme cold weather sharply raises the consumption of oil for heating in Country A
C. The government of Country A provides tax relief for research in new energy aiming to lessen the reliance on oil 
D. An earthquake destroys an oil refinery factory in Country A
甲國是石油進口國。下列哪項會令其短期總供應曲線向右移？
A. 所有石油出口國決定增加每日產油量B. 極端寒冷天氣令甲國大幅增加耗油作保暖用途
C. 甲國政府為減低對石油的依賴，而對新能源的研究提供稅務減免D. 一場地震破壞了甲國的一座煉油廠
Example Question 28 DSE2019MCQ38
The government carried out the following two policies:
Policy one :
Reduce the profits tax rate for corporations with profit less than two million dollars.
Policy two : 
Inject fifty billion dollars to develop innovation and technology.
Which of the AD-AS diagrams could most likely show the effect on aggregate output caused by the above two policies?
政府實施下列兩項政策：
政策一：
減低盈利低於二百萬元的公司的之利得稅率
政策二：
投入五百億元，發展創新科技
下列哪幅總供需圖最能顯示上述兩項政策對總產出的影響？
Example Question 29 DSE2013MCQ23
The aggregate demand curve will shift to the right if 
A. there is a fall in the price level B. there is an improveme

a fall in the price level B. there is an improvement in technology 
C. the government reduces the import tariff D. the government reduces the salaries tax rate
在下列哪種情況下，總需求曲線會向右移？
A. 物價水平下降B. 科技進步
C. 政府減低進口關稅D. 政府減低薪俸稅率
Example Question 30 DSE2020MCQ27
Which of the following is a reason for a leftward shift of the short run aggregate supply curve in a country?
A. There is a continuous increase in price level
B. The largest market of the country's exports suffers from an economic recession.
C. More citizens emigrate to foreign countries.
D. The wages of labour slowly adjust downward due to institutional factors.
下列哪項是某國短期總供應曲線向左移的原因？
A. 價格水平持續上升B. 該國的最大出口市場遭逢經濟衰退
C. 更多公民移居外國D. 由於制度因素，勞工的工資緩慢下調
Example Question 31 DSE2021MCQ27
Which of the following is a possible reason of the above shift of the short run aggregate supply curve?
A. The cost of production increases due to an increase in global demand for oil.
B. The profitability of firms increases with the rise in price level, since the difference between outpu

in price level, since the difference between output prices and input prices increases.
C. An increase in interest rate leads to an increase in cost of investment.
D. The operation of labour market resumes to normal after a massive labour strike.
下列哪項是導致短期總供應曲線作以上移動的一個可能原因？
A. 全球對石油的需求上升，導致生產成本上升
B. 公司的利潤隨著物價水平上升而增加，因為產出價格和投入價格之間的差距擴大
C. 利率上升，令投資的成本上升
D. 在一次大規模罷工後，勞工市場回復正常運作
Example Question 32 DSE2021MCQ29
The outbreak of COVID-19 results in a significant decline in employment level and the volume of exports of a country. Which of the following policies can most likely relieve the above two problems?
A. The Central bank reduces the discount rate.
B. The government distributes cash to citizens aged 18 years old or above.
C. The government imposes "employment support scheme' to industries that are adversely affected.
D. The country devalues its currency against the currencies of its major trading partners.
新冠肺炎的爆發令某國的就業水平和出口數量顯著下跌。下列哪項政策最能舒緩以上兩個問題？
A. 中央銀行調低貼現率
B. 政府向18歲或以上的市民派發現金
C. 政府對受負面影響的行業實行「就業支援計劃」
D. 該國

B. 政府向18歲或以上的市民派發現金
C. 政府對受負面影響的行業實行「就業支援計劃」
D. 該國令其貨幣兌主要貿易夥伴的貨幣官方貶值
Example Question 33 DSE2021MCQ36
Due to the COVID-19 outbreak, most of the flights were cancelled, leading to a significant drop in the number of tourists visiting Hong Kong. At the same time, many employees in Hong Kong were forced to take no pay leave. Which of the following diagrams can best describe the consequence of the above incidents?
由於新冠肺炎爆發，大部分航班取消，引致訪港旅客人數大減。同時，本港很多僱員被迫放無薪假期。下列哪圖最能描述上述事件的後果？
Type 2 (Change in ADAS with inflationary gap and deflationary gap)
Example Question 1 DSEPPMCQ32
Which of the following can help narrow a deflationary (output) gap?
A. an increase in the required reserve ratioB. a sale of bonds by the central bank in the market 
C. an increase in government expenditureD. the abolishment of a tariff on imports
下列哪項有助收窄通縮(產出)差距？
A. 法定儲備比率增加。                   B. 中央銀行在市場售賣債券
C. 政府支出增加                         D. 撤銷進口關稅
Example Question 2 DSE2012MCQ26
The diagram below shows the aggregate demand curves, the short

elow shows the aggregate demand curves, the short run aggregate supply curves and the long run aggregate supply curve of a country. Point E is the initial equilibrium point. 
下圖顯示某國的總需求(AD)曲線，短期總供應(SRAS)曲線以及長期總供應(LRAS)曲線。E點是起初的均衡點。
When there is an economic recovery in the country’s major trading partner, ______ in the short run and _______ in the long run.
A. AD1 will shift to AD2 ... SRAS1 will shift to SRAS2B. AD1 will shift to AD2 ... AD2 will shift back to AD1
C. SARS1 will shift to SRAS2 ... AD1 will shift to AD2D. SARS1 will shift to SRAS2 ... SRAS2 will shift back to SRAS1

Marking Scheme:
(a)Wealth effect / Net export effect/ (Suggested Answer) Interest rate effect:
As the price level rises, households and firms demand more money to finance their transactions. Given the fixed supply of money, the interest rate would rise. An increase in interest rate would cause decline in investment and consumption and also the real output. (3)
財富效應 / 淨出口效應 /（建議答案）利率效應
利率效應：當物價水平上升，住戶和公司需求更多的貨幣為其交易融資。已知貨幣供應是固定的，利率會上升。
利率的上升會令投資和消費下跌，實質產出下跌。
(b)Increase in the price of raw materials results in a rise in f

the price of raw materials results in a rise in factor prices. So the SRAS will decrease. PL increases and the output will fall in the SR. The resulting excess supply of labour / deflationary (output) gap will result in a downward pressure on the input prices. In the LR, wages will adjust downward and the SRAS will increase and the PL will fall back to the original level. (5)
SRAS shifts to the left-PL increases, Y drops
SRAS shifts back to the original position -Final fall in P, back to the original level    (4)
原材料價格上升，令生產要素價格上升。因此SRAS會下跌。在短期，物價水平上升而產出會下降。所引起的勞工市場的超額供應 / 通縮(產出)差距會令投入的價格由下調壓力。在長期，工資會向下調整。而SRAS會上升，物價水平下降至原初水平。(5)
     在圖中顯示：
SRAS向左移    -P上升，Y下跌  
SRAS移回原初位置   -P最後回到原初水平(4)

people at school) and thus aggregate output in the current period. (2)
Proposal 2:
Unemployment benefits would increase the disposable income and consumption of the jobless, and thus aggregate demand, resulting in a rise in aggregate output. 
Unemployment benefits may, however, produce disincentive effects on labor supply, resulting in de-skilling of labour (i.e. decumulation of human capital) and a drop in aggregate output now and in the future.(2)
方案一：
機會均等：免費教育的年期增加令窮人能接受更多的教育，從而提高他們未來的生產力和賺錢能力。
其他因素不變，在教育上花費更多，會增加政府指出，引致總需求和總產出上升。
免費教育的年期增加會提高勞工生產力，並增加日後的總產出。
對正常就學年齡意外的人士提供免費教育，會令勞力供應下降(更多人留在學校)，故此現時的總產出下降。
方案二：
收入 / 結果均等：給予失業人士較多福利縮少就業人士(富人)和失業人士(窮人)的收入差距。
失業援助金會令失業者的可用收入和消費增加，總需求上升，導致總產出增加。
失業援助金可能對勞力供應有抑制作用，令現在和將來勞力的技術水平下降(即人力資本消減)及總產出下跌。

Marking Scheme:
Proposal A’s effect on aggregate output:
AD drops because disposable income drops and thus consumption drops.  As a result, output drops. 
[SRAS might drop due to the disincentive effect on working incentive of the tax](2)
Proposal B’s effect on aggregate output:
There will be no extra effect on aggregate output as there is no change in AD/AS components.(2)

增加薪俸稅令AD下降，因為可用收入下降而令消費下降。產出因而下降。

提案B對總產出的影響：
因為AD / AS的組成部份沒有轉變，對總產出不會出現額外的影響。               (2)

DSE2012 LQ11cd
Read the following news extract. 
Hong Kong’s inflation rate rose to 7.9% last month – the sharpest jump in 16 years – confirming what many families already feared as they struggle to keep up with the runway cost of food and rent.
To relieve the effect of inflation on citizens, the government has issued inflation-linked bonds (ibonds) with interest paid to bondholders every six months. The interest rate is positively related to the actual inflation rate of the last half-year period. 
(a)What is the opportunity cost of holding cash instead of ibonds?(2)
(b)Explain how ibonds would relieve the effect of inflation on their holders.(2)
細閱下列的新聞節錄。
香港通脹率在上月份上升至7.9%——為16年來最大的升幅——確定了許多家庭所懼怕的情況：他們為應付飛漲的食物成本和租金而感到吃力。
為紓緩通脹對市民的影響，政府發行了與通脹掛鈎的債券（ibonds），每六個月向債券持有人支付利息。其利率與過去半年期的實際通脹率有正向關係。
(a) 持有現金而不持有與通脹掛鈎的債券的機會成本是什麼？(2)
(b) 解釋與通脹掛鈎的債券會如何紓緩通脹對其擁有人的影響。(2)
_______________________________________________________________________________________
____________________________

metadata.source: Ch 19 Money Market PP 4.0 (wait double check).docx
Ch 19 Money Market PP 4.0 (wait double check).docx

chunks.text:
_______________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
Marking Scheme:
(a)The opportunity cost is the nominal return of the ibonds.(2)
(b)The ibonds help preserve the purchasing power of the invested sum of money because the interest return of ibonds will increase at the same rate as actual inflation.(2)
(a) 該機會成本是與通脹掛鈎的債券的名義回報。(2)
(b) 與通脹掛鈎的債券幫助保存所投資的款項的購買力。因為該債券的利息回報率的增加會和實際通脹率的增加
同。(2)
DSE2013 LQ3ab
(a)What is the relationship between nominal interest rate and real interest rate?(2)
(b)“When there is actual deflation, the nominal interest rate will be lower than the real interest rate.” Do you agree? Explain.(3)
名義利率與實質利率有什麼關係？(2)
「當實際通貨收縮出現，名義利率會低於實質利率。」你同意嗎？試加解釋。(3)
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________

metadata.source: Ch 19 Money Market PP 4.0 (wait double check).docx
Ch 19 Money Market PP 4.0 (wait double check).docx

chunks.text:
________
Marking Scheme:
(a)Nominal interest rate (R) = expected inflation rate + real interest rate (r)(2)
(b)No, R will be lower than r only when the expected inflation rate is negative. But the existence of actual deflation does not imply that the deflation is expected. /(3)
Yes, because people may expect deflation beforehand and expected deflation will result in a nominal interest rate being lower than real interest rate. /(3)
Yes, because the realized (actual) real interest rate will be higher than the nominal interest rate when there is actual deflation. (3)
(a)名義利率(R)=預期通脹率+實質利率 (r)(2)
(b)不會，因為只有當預期通脹率是負數時，名義利率(R)低於實質利率(r)。實際通縮的出現並不意味著通縮是預期
的。/(3)
會。因為人們可能預期了通縮出現，而預期通縮會導致名義利率低於實質利率。/(3)
會。因為當實際通縮出現，實際的實質利率會高於名義利率。(3)
Marking Scheme:
Nominal interest (rate)(1)
Because holding money gives up the opportunity to deposit the sum to banks and earn interest income. (1)
名義利息（或利率）(1)
因為某人持有貨幣時便放棄了將該筆金錢存入銀行並賺取利息收入的機會，或將該筆金錢購買可賺取利息的資產（如債券）的機會。(1)

emain unchanged as long as trade continues

在乙國，生產每單位C的機會成本=8R/4C=2R       
因此甲國(乙國)生產R(C)有比較優勢。 
乙國每單位貿易得益= 4R-2R =2R
總貿易得益= (4R-2R) X 3 =6R或
換言之，乙國會是成衣生產國，生產4C，出口3C以換取12R並保留1C作本國消費之用。
沒有貿易時，乙國可以自行生產1C和解6R。它的總貿易得益是6R(=12R-6R)

Ch1.4Scarcity稀少性
Definition:
Exists when the quantity available of resources is insufficient to satisfy all human wants
定義：
當可用資源的數量不足以滿足所有人類的慾望時，稀少性便存在
Can exist in one-man economy and barter economy 
可以存在於一人經濟和以物易物的經濟中
May not be solved by money 
(rich people still face the problem of scarcity)
未必用錢就能解決的
（即便是富人還是會面臨稀少性的問題）
May not exist whenever the want of goods is large / great demand
當物品需求很大時，亦未必存在
May not exist whenever the quantity available of goods is very small 
當可用的物品數量很少時，亦未必存在
Imply there is a cost in obtain some goods 
意味獲取該物品會涉及成本
Scarcity does not mean unlimited human want or limited resources 
it means unlimited human wants and limited resources 
稀少性不代表無限的人類慾望 / 有限的資源 
它意味著無限的人類慾望及有限的資源
Supply may not be fixed
供應未必是固定的
Even choice is made, there is still scarcity
即使已作選擇，稀少性仍然存在
Unlimited Want  Limited R

scarcity
即使已作選擇，稀少性仍然存在
Unlimited Want  Limited Resources  Scarcity
Due to scarcity, we need to make choice  Cost  Competition  Discrimination

1.7Opportunity Cost 機會成本
Type 6: Concept of full cost 全部成本的概念
Opportunity Cost = Money cost + Non-money cost 
Money cost = The

t = Money cost + Non-money cost 
Money cost = The payment of money
Non-money cost = The highest-valued alternative use of resources (time)
Example:
Crystal and Jason love Pakho so much and decide to buy the concert tickets. Both of them spend 24hrs to queue and use $1,000 to buy the ticket.
Do they necessarily share the same opportunity cost?
Answer:
No, although their money cost is the same as $1,000, their non-money cost may not be not the same.
Crystal may use the 24 hours to earn $2000 as a tutor while Jason may only earn $800 as a KFC worker.
Their alternative use of time is not the same.  
Tips:
Different people have different time cost 
∵ their alternative use of time is not the same
Some forgo 1 hour may earn $50 while some forgo 1 jour may earn $5,000… 
Even the time forgone is the same the time cost is different
機會成本 ＝ 金錢成本 ＋ 非金錢成本
金錢成本 ＝ 所支付的金錢
非金錢成本 ＝ 資源的最高價值的替代用途（時間）
例子：
Crystal、Jason 很喜歡周柏豪，所以決定購買他的演唱會門票。他們同樣花了 24 小時排隊並且花了 $1,000 購買門票。
他們的機會成本是否必定相同？
答案：
否，雖然他們的金錢成本同樣是 $1,000，但非金錢成本未必一樣。
Crystal

定相同？
答案：
否，雖然他們的金錢成本同樣是 $1,000，但非金錢成本未必一樣。
Crystal 可能用該 24 小時來做補習老師賺取$2,000，而 Jason 則只可以做肯德基員工取$800。
他們各自的時間替代用途是不相同的。
Tips:
不同人有不同時間成本
∵ 他們的時間替代用途不一樣
一些人放棄1小時只可賺$50而一些人放棄1小時則可賺$5,000…
即使放棄的時間一樣，其時間成本未必一樣
1.7Opportunity Cost 機會成本
Type 7: More details about Time Cost 時間成本的詳細
Example:
Janice decides to go to watch a tennis competition for 3 hours while she gets the ticket from his dad without paying any money to his dad. 
How will the opportunity cost be affected if the tennis competition will be overrun by 30 minutes due to the overtime match?
Total time to be spent for the tennis competition increase
The highest-valued alternative use of time increase
The opportunity cost increase 
How will the opportunity cost be affected if Janice is late for the tennis competition for 2 hours due to the traffic jam?
Total time to be spent on the competition remains unchanged
The highest-valued alternative use of the time remains unchanged
The opportunity cost remains unchanged
Is the cost of watching the tennis competition

ged
Is the cost of watching the tennis competition zero?
No, the opportunity cost of watching the tennis competition is the highest-valued alternative use of time (3 hours), which maybe not the same for different people.
Is the cost of watching the tennis competition decrease if Yao Ming is sit in front of Janice so that she cannot watch the competition?
No, the opportunity cost remains unchanged.
The time cost and money cost remain unchanged, only the value of watching tennis competition decrease. 
例子： 
Janice 決定去看一場 3 小時的網球比賽，她從他爸爸那裡拿到了門票，但沒有付任何錢給他爸爸。 
如果網球比賽因加時賽而超時 30 分鐘，機會成本將如何受到影響？ 
網球比賽總時間增加 
最高價值的替代時間用途增加 
機會成本增加 
如果 Janice 由於交通堵塞在網球比賽中遲到 2 小時，機會成本將如何受到影響？ 
比賽總時間保持不變 
時間的最高價值替代用途保持不變
機會成本不變 
觀看網球比賽的成本是零嗎？ 
不，觀看網球比賽的機會成本是價值最高的時間替代用途（3小時），對於不同人可能不一樣。 
如果姚明坐在 Janice 前面使她不能觀看比賽，觀看網球比賽的成本是否會降低？
不，機會成本保持不變。 
時間成本和金錢成本不變，只有看網球比賽的價值減少。

1.7Opportunity Cost 機會成本
Type 9: More about the non-money cost 續：非金錢成本
Example
Health Cost:
Explain why the cost of playing basketball increase during rainy day / when the basketball field is damaged?
The risk of getting hurt or getting sick increase during rainy day / when the basketball field is damaged.
The non-money cost increase.
Thus, the opportunity cost increase.
Environmental cost:
Constructing a highspeed rail between China and Hone Kong is $5 billion. Explain why the cost of constructing highspeed rail is more than $5 billion?
The construction of the highspeed rail may lead to noise and land pollution to the area nearby
The non-money cost increase.
Thus, the opportunity cost increase.
例子 
i) 健康成本：
解釋為什麼在雨天 / 籃球場損壞時打籃球的成本會增加？ 
下雨天 / 籃球場損壞時打籃球受傷或生病的風險增加。 
非金錢成本增加。 
因此，機會成本增加。 
ii) 環境成本： 
在中國和香港之間建設高鐵耗資 $50 億。解釋為什麼建設高鐵的成本超過 $50 億元？ 
高鐵建設可能對附近地區造成噪音和土地污染 
非金錢成本增加。 
因此，機會成本增加。
1.7Opportunity Cost 機會成本
Type 10: Sunk Cost / Historical cost 沉沒成本/歷史成本
Sunk cost is past expend

Historical cost 沉沒成本/歷史成本
Sunk cost is past expenditure only
Resources spent in the past is irrecoverable and we do not count the cost we paid in the past
Example:
Suppose Ronson buy the ticket of Wong Tsz Wa stand-up comedy at $5,000 on 16, May 2018 
There are three people want to get the ticket at $500, $ 2,000 and $3,000 now.
Since the buying price is not an option available at present and only a past expenditure.
The opportunity cost is the highest-valued option forgone which is $3,000.
Tips:
***Important reminder:
If there are no any other choices, the cost will be zero. Since nothing is given up when making a choice.
If there is only one person in the world, opportunity cost still exists.
Money and time themselves are not an opportunity cost, only the highest-valued alternative use of the money and time is the opportunity cost. 
Conclusion: 
Opportunity cost will change when 
value of highest-valued option forgone changes.
monetary / time / non-monetary cost of chosen option changes.
Opportunity cost wi

cost of chosen option changes.
Opportunity cost will NOT change when 
cost of highest-valued option forgone changes.
value of chosen option changes 
沉沒成本只是過去的支出 
過去花費的資源是不可挽回的，不應影響決策，我們不會計算過去付出的成本 
例子： 
假設 Ronson 於2018年5月16日以$5,000購買黃子華棟篤笑的門票 
現在有3個人分別想買$500、$2,000和$3,000的票。 
由於先前購買的價格不是目前可以選擇的選項，只是過去的支出。 
機會成本是放棄的價值最高的選項，即 $3,000 。 
Tips： 
***重要提示： 
如果沒有任何其他選擇，成本將為零。因為在做出選擇時沒有放棄任何東西。 
即使世界上只有一個人，機會成本依然存在。 
金錢和時間本身不是機會成本，只有金錢和時間中最高價值替代用途才是機會成本。 
結論： 
機會成本會改變，如果：
價值最高的放棄選項改變了價值。
所選選項的金錢 / 時間 / 非金錢成本改變。
機會成本不會改變，如果：
價值最高的放棄選項的成本改變 
所選選項的價值改變
1.8More About Interest and Opportunity Cost 利息 & 機會成本
Definition:
To borrower, interest is the cost of earlier availability of resources
To lender, interest is the return / compensation / premium for postponing current consumption
Interest is both cost and return, depends on the borrower or lender
Interest rate increase  cost of borrowing increase  borrowing decrease (vice versa)
Example:
Rex does not have enough money to buy a flat in central. He borrows money from HSBC ba

y a flat in central. He borrows money from HSBC bank with an interest rate of 3% to buy the flat.
To Rex (borrower), interest of 3% is the cost to get the flat earlier. 
To HSBC (lender), interest of 3% is the return for delaying the use of the money. 
People are willing to pay a price for early consumption because
Earlier availability is preferred to later availability
Value of a good now > Value of an identical good in the future
Current consumption is preferred to future consumption 
They are impatient to wait
A dollar today worth more than a dollar tomorrow 
Important reminder:
Interest can exist without money / in barter economy
People are impatient to wait, they will pay a price for earlier availability 
Interest exists whenever there is a choice between current consumption and future consumption
Without money, interest can be express in term of good
For example, you borrow 10 pencils and returns for 12 pencils later 
定義： 
對於借方(債務人)來說，利息是提前獲得資源的成本 
對於貸方(債權人)來說，利息是延遲當前消費的回報 / 補償 / 溢價 
利息既是成本又是回報，取決於是借款人或

債權人)來說，利息是延遲當前消費的回報 / 補償 / 溢價 
利息既是成本又是回報，取決於是借款人或貸款人 
利率上升  借貸成本增加  借貸減少（反之亦然） 
例子：
Rex 沒有足夠的錢在中環買一間屋。他以3%的利率向匯豐銀行借貸置業。 
對Rex（借方）來說，3%的利息是提前拿到物業的成本。 
對HSBC（貸方）來說，3%的利息是延遲使用該筆款項的回報。 
人們願意為早期消費付出代價，因為:
早用勝於遲用
現在商品的價值 > 未來相同商品的價值 
當前消費勝於未來消費 
人們缺乏耐性 
今天的一元比明天的一元更值錢 
重要提示：
利息可以在沒有貨幣 / 在以物易物經濟下存在 
人們缺乏耐性，他們會為了更早享用物品付出溢價 
要在當前消費和未來消費之間做出選擇，就會產生利息 
即使沒有貨幣，利息亦可以用物品來表達
例如，你借了 10 支鉛筆，然後歸還12 支鉛筆

***MIND THE KEY WORDS***
What to produce? 生產甚麼？
The choice of the type and quantities goods and services to produce

E.g.The Apple store decides to produce more IPhone 8 and cut the amount of IPhone X (quantity)

E.g. Apple 決定生產 Ipod、Iphone、Ipad、Iwatch、Airpod、Apple TV……（類型） 
E.g. Apple 決定增產iPhone 8並減產iPhone X的數量（數量）
How to produce? 如何生產？
The choice of production methods 
usually the least costly

E.g. Handmade method 手工製 vs Machine method 機械製

For whom to produce? 為誰生產？
The choice of distribution of goods and services 
Setting the criteria to decide who have the right of the product / services
  物品及服務的分配選擇
  為誰可有權使用物品或服務制定條件 
E.g.Distribution criteria 分配條件
1.Price competition 價格競爭2.Non-price competition 非價格競爭
E.g.Increase the price 加價 E.g.First come first serve 先到先得
Provide discounts 折扣    By examination / academics result 考試 / 成績

Ch1.10Different Economic Systems不同的經濟制度 / 經濟體系
Traditional economy
The three problems are mainly tackled by social custom and traditions.
E.g. Only the eldest males have the right to build village houses
Planned / Command economy
Most resources are owned by the government 
The three problems are mainly tackled by the government’s central decision and planning 
E.g.Policy is imposed according to the government
Market economy
Most resources are privately owned

Market economy
Most resources are privately owned
The three problems are mainly tackled by the price mechanism / price signal 
Without the private property right system, market price cannot be used to tackle the three problems  
Private property rights 
Exclusive right to use
Exclusive right to receive income
Right to transfer
If there is no clear definition of private property right, no one will be willing to buy the product since even he buys it, the public may still use it. 
Mixed Economy
No society is ONLY a traditional, planned or market economy 
In reality, all economies are mixed economies
1. 傳統經濟
這三個問題主要靠社會習俗和傳統來解決 
Eg. 只有最年長的男性才有權建造村屋 
2. 計劃經濟 / 指令經濟
大多數資源歸政府所有 
三大問題主要由政府中央決策部署解決 
E.g. 政策是根據政府實施的 
3. 市場經濟 
大部分資源為私人所有 
三大問題主要通過價格機制 / 價格信號來解決
如沒有私有產權制度，市場價格無法解決這三個問題 
私有產權 
獨有使用權
獨有收入權
轉讓權
如果沒有明確的私有產權定義，就沒有人願意購買這個產品，因為即使他購買了，公眾所有人仍然可以使用它。 
4. 混合經濟 
沒有一個社會僅僅是傳統、計劃或市場經濟 
現實中，所有經濟體系都是混合經濟
1.11Economy Activities經濟活動
Consumption
The process of utilizing resources to directly satisfy human wants
E.g.Ken buys

rces to directly satisfy human wants
E.g.Ken buys an apple for dinner 
Production
The process of transforming inputs into output to create value 
E.g.Jason buys an apple for making an apple pie in his own bakery 
Specialization
Producers focus on producing an output or a particular production stage of an output
Different people / economies concentrate on producing different goods.
E.g. Henry specializes in teaching Liberal Studies while Herman specializes in teaching Mathematics.
Exchange
People voluntarily trade output with others
*****Important reminder*****
Exchange is a necessary condition for specialization. 
Without exchange, people cannot consume all the goods they want
They have to produce whatever they need instead of producing certain goods
There will be no specialization. 
E.g.Japan specialize on producing cup noodles while Thailand specialize on producing rice and they exchange with each other.
1. 消費 
利用資源直接滿足人類慾望的過程 
E.g. Ken買了一個蘋果作為晚飯
2. 生產 
將投入轉化為產出以創造價值的過程 
E.g. Jason在自己的麵包店裡買了一個蘋果來做蘋果批 
3. 專門

化為產出以創造價值的過程 
E.g. Jason在自己的麵包店裡買了一個蘋果來做蘋果批 
3. 專門化
生產者專注於生產某個商品或商品某個特定的生產階段 
不同的人 / 經濟專注於生產不同的商品。 
E.g. Henry專門教授通識教育，而Herman專門教授數學。 
4. 貿易 
人們自願與他人交易產出 
*****重要提示***** 
貿易是專門化的必要條件。 
沒有貿易，人們就無法消費他們想要的所有商品 
他們必須生產他們需要的任何東西，而不是只生產某些物品 
不會有專門化。 
E.g. 日本專門化生產杯麵，泰國專門化生產大米，然後進行貿易。
1.12Positive, Normative Statement and Tautology 實證性陳述、規範性陳述、套套邏輯
Positive statement 
Does not involve value judgement / personal preferences 
Testable and refutable
About fact
Not must be true 
E.g.The Gini coefficient of Hong Kong is 0.531.
E.g.He is watching television.
Normative statement
Involve value judgement / personal preferences
Not testable and refutable 
About what should be or what ought to be 
Usually involve some words (should / need to / ought to / I think etc…)
E.g.Emily thinks Ken is ugly
E.g.The government should increase the progressivity of the tax to even the income distribution.
Tautology
Must be true & Not refutable 
Theory / Definition
E.g. When the price increases, the quantity demanded decreases, assume other t

s, the quantity demanded decreases, assume other things being constant. 
E.g.Circle is round shape 
1. 實證性陳述 
不涉及價值判斷 / 個人喜好 
可被事實推翻及可反駁 
關於事實 
不一定是真確的 
E.g. 香港的堅尼系數為0.531。 
E.g. 他正在看電視。 
2. 規範性陳述 
涉及價值判斷 / 個人喜好 
不可實證及反駁 
關於應該 / 不應該的問題
通常涉及一些字眼（應該 / 需要 / 應該 / 我認為 / 覺得...） 
E.g.  Emily覺得Ken很醜 
E.g.  政府應提高稅收的累進程度，以均衡收入分配。 
3. 套套邏輯 (恆真句 / 同義反覆)
必定真確且不可反駁 
理論 / 定義 
E.g. 當價格上升時，需求量減少，假設其他因素不變。
E.g. 圓形是圓的
1.13Microeconomics and Macroeconomics 微觀、宏觀經濟學
Microeconomics: a study of individual economics units
Macroeconomics: a study of the performance of an economy as a whole
微觀經濟學: 研究經濟個體
宏觀經濟學: 研究經濟整體表現

Factor market

Market for buying and selling goods and services  household buy goods and servic

goods and services  household buy goods and services
1. 廠商 
生產單位  使用生產要素生產物品和服務 
2. 住戶 
消費單位  提供要素服務，賺取消費收入

生產要素的買賣市場  企業僱用員工（如勞工市場） 
4. 產品市場 
買賣商品和服務的市場  家庭購買商品和服務

(b)在「搭十送一」計下，乘客在一週內星期一至五付款乘搭十程港鐵，便能獲得免費單程車票一張。該免費的車票是否免費物品？試加解釋。                                      (2)

Marking Scheme:
(a)Private property rights refer to the exclusive right to use, the exclusive right to receive income from, and the right to transfer a property owned by an individual. The private property rights over the flats under the scheme are weakened as the flat-owners’ right of selling (or transferring) their flats to non-residents is confined.(4)
(b)No, as the provision of MTR service involves the use of scarce resources having alternative uses. (2)
(c)No, as people have different alternative uses of time, their time costs will not be the same.(3)
(a)私有產權是指對私人擁有産業的獨有使用權、收入專屬權及轉讓權。在計劃下樓宇的私有產權被削弱了，原因是樓宇的擁有人將其單位賣給非本地居民的權利被限制了。 (4)
(b)否，因為提供港鐵服務須運用稀少及有其他用途的資源。(2)
(c)否，

的權利被限制了。 (4)
(b)否，因為提供港鐵服務須運用稀少及有其他用途的資源。(2)
(c)否，因為人們時間的其他用途各有不同，他們的時間成本不盡相同。(3)
2014DSE LQ1a, b 
Serine is a university graduate who is seeking a job. She has several options and her order of preference is shown below:
First preference:      to work for the government as an administrative officer 
Second preference:   to work in an accounting firm as a trainee
Third preference:     to continues to seek a job without accepting any offers.
Analyse whether Serine’s opportunity cost of choosing to work as an administrative officer will necessarily remain unchanged if 
(a)The government reduces the starting salary of administrative officers (2)
(b)The government provides unemployment benefits for all graduates seeking jobs.(3)
小玲是一位正在找工作的大學畢業生。她有數個選擇，而她的優先次序如下列所示：
第一選擇：在政府任職政務主任
第二選擇：在會計師行任職見習生。
第三選擇：繼續找工作，暫時不接受任何工作。
在以下的情況中，分析小玲選擇仼職政務主仼的機會成本會否必然維持不變：
(a)政府調低政務主仼的起始薪金。                                               (2)
(b)政府向所有正在找工作的畢業生提供失業援助金。                                (3)
__________________________________

Marking Scheme:
(a)Yes.(1)
It is because the reduction in the starting salary of administrative officers would only decrease the value of Serine’s chosen option without affecting her highest valued option forgone.(1)
(b)No.(1)
Government provision of unemployment benefits may increase the value of Serine’s 3rd option to a level higher than that of the 2nd option. So the cost of choosing to work as an administrative officer may now change from the value of the 2nd option (i.e. to work in an accounting firm as a trainee) to that of the 3rd option (i.e. to continue to seek a job without taking up any job and simply enjoying the unemployment benefit)(2)
(a)是。                                                                    (1)
因為政務主仼起始薪金下跌只會令小玲已選擇選項的價值下跌，而不會影響在放棄的選項中價最高者。  (1)                                                                 
(b)否。                                                                                      (1)
由於政府提供的失業援助金可能令小玲第三選項的價值上升至超越第二選項的價值的水平。所以選擇任職政務主任的成本可能從第二選項的價值（即在會計師樓任職實習生）變為

越第二選項的價值的水平。所以選擇任職政務主任的成本可能從第二選項的價值（即在會計師樓任職實習生）變為第三選項的價值（即繼續找工作而沒有接受任何工作並亨有失業援助金）。                         (2)

Because it has positive marginal cost of production. or
Because it is produced from scare resources, which have alternative uses.(1)
(b)Yes,(1)
Because a person can download and study the course materials without reducing the availability of those materials to other, i.e. these materials are non-rivalrous in consumption.(2)
(c)No, (1)
Because the time cost of a person taking MOOC may be higher than the time cost of another person taking on campus course plus the course fee; or
The former person (MOOC student) may have to spend extra time to access / download the course materials and to communicate with the course instructor or teaching assistant; or
The former person may also have to pay for internet connection in order to access the course materials.(2)
(a)否，                                                                   (1)
     因為該物品邊際生產成本是大於零。或

(1)
     因為該物品邊際生產成本是大於零。或                                    (1)
     因為該物品是運用稀少的資源所生產，而這些資源有其他用途。
(b)是，                                                                    (1)
     因為一個人可下載並學習該教材時，並沒減少另一人可獲取該教材的機會，即非競爭性消費。(2)
(c)否，                                                                    (1)
     因為某人報讀MOOC的時間成本可能高於另一人報讀校內課程的時間成本加上該課程學費。
    或  前者（MOOC學生）可能須花額外的時間獲取 / 下載該教材及和課程講師或教學助理溝通。
    或  前者可能要支付接駁互聯網的費用以獲取該教材。   (2)
DSE 2016 LQ 1
Apple Inc. launches a new model of smartphone. In order to buy the new model, some people spend hours in trying to login the website of Apple Inc. If the resale value of the new model falls, the opportunity cost of buying the new model through the website of Apple Inc. will decrease. Do you agree? Explain your answer.(3)
蘋果公司推出新型號的智能手機。為了購買該新型號手機，有人花費數小時嘗試登入蘋果公司的網站。若該新型號手機的轉售價值下跌，透過蘋果公司網站購買該新型號手機的機會成本會下跌。你同意嗎？解釋你的答案。(3)
____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

rking Scheme:
The full cost of buying the new model includes the price of new model (monetary cost) plus the time spent logging in the website.(1)
The resale value of the new model is not a forgone option.(1)
So the cost of buying the new model does not change.(1)
Or
The drop in resale value will reduce the (speculators’) demand for the new model and(1)
Therefore, reduce the excess demand,(1)
Lowering the time spent logging in the website and reduce the time cost of buying the new model.(1)
購買新型號手機的全部成本包括新型號手機的價格（金錢成本）加上登入網站所花時間的價值。               (1)
新型號手機的轉售價值不是所放棄的選項。                                       (1)
故此當該價值下降購買新型號手機的成本没有改變。                               (1)
或
轉售價值的下降會減低（炒家）對新型號手機的需求。                             (1)
因此超額需求下降。                                                         (1)
登入網站所需的時間減少，購買新型號的時間成本下跌，購買新型號手機的成本下跌。(1)

1
Although air ticket fares and hotel rates are usually higher during holiday periods, many people still travel abroad. Explain, in terms of opportunity cost, why it is not necessarily more expensive for travelers to travel abroad during holiday periods than non-holiday periods.(3)
雖然機票和酒店的費用在假日期間通常比較高，但是很多人仍然會到外地旅遊。以機會成本為依據，解釋為什麼旅客在假日期間到外地旅遊未必比非假日期間昂貴。                  (3)
___________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________________
Marking Scheme:
No, Full cost = monetary cost + time cost
During holiday periods, although the monetary cost of travelling abroad (including air ticket fares and hotel expenses) is usually higher than that duri

d hotel expenses) is usually higher than that during non-holiday periods, the time cost (e.g. income forgone) may be a lot lower. If the time-cost saved outweighs the extra monetary cost, then in terms of full cost travelling abroad during holiday periods would be less expensive.(3)
全部成本 = 金錢成本 + 時間成本
雖然在假日期間到外地旅遊的金錢成本 (包括機票和酒店費用) 一般較非假日為高，但是假日期間的時間成本 (例如所放棄的收人) 可能低很多。若所節省的時間成本高於額外的金錢成本，依全部成本計算，假日期間外遊會較便宜。(3)

 Students are expected to 
 State the law of demand, and 
 explain phenomena by applying the law of demand
(using relative price or full price), such as how an imposition of lump-sum fee alters the relative price

position of lump-sum fee alters the relative price between two goods. 
[Note: Students are NOT expected to use the term
“Alchian’s Generalisation”.]
 Students are expected to 
understand the meaning of complements, substitutes, superior and inferior goods, and
explain how price of related goods, income, price expectation, and weather, etc. affect individual demand. 
 Students are expected to distinguish between change in demand (caused by changes in exogeneous variables) and change in quantity demanded (caused by changes in an endogenous variable, i.e. the price of the good).
 Students are expected to
understand that market demand could be obtained by horizontal summation of individual demand for a private good,
understand the meaning of horizontal summation, and
be able to add up the individual demand curves / schedules horizontally to obtain the market demand curve / schedule.
 Students are expected to explain how price of related goods, income, price expectation, weather, and number of consumers in the mar

ation, weather, and number of consumers in the market, etc. affect market demand.
 Students are expected to 
state the law of supply 
explain how price of related goods, cost of production, technology, price expectation and weather, etc. affect individual supply, and
understand the meaning of joint supply and competitive 
supply. 
 Students are expected to distinguish between change in supply (caused by changes in exogenous variables) and change in quantity supplied (caused by changes in an endogenous variable, i.e. the price of the good). 
 Students are expected to be able to add up the individual supply curves / schedules horizontally to obtain the market supply curve / schedule.
Students are expected to explain how price of related goods, cost of production, technology, price expectation, weather, and number of sellers in the market, etc. affect market supply.
 Students are expected to identify the
equilibrium price and quantity graphically, and
equilibrium price and quantity, given the demand and supply s

price and quantity, given the demand and supply schedules. 
 Students are expected to explain the effects of change in demand and/or change in supply on equilibrium price and quantity, with the aid of diagram(s).

2.8 Demand, Supply vs Quantity Demand, Quantity Supplied Concept Corner:

2.9 Relative Price相對價格
Example:
Airline ticket to Taiwan is $2,000 & Airline ticket to England is $10,000
The relative price of an airline ticket to TW in terms of airline ticket to UK
= $2,000 ÷ $10,000= 0.2 airline ticket to England
The relative price of an airline ticket to UK in terms of airline ticket to TW
= $10,000 ÷ $2,000= 5 airline tickets to Taiwan
If there is an additional fuel surcharge $2,000
The relative price of an airline ticket to TW in terms of airline ticket to UK 
= $4,000 ÷ $12,000= 0.33 airline ticket to England
The relative price of an airline ticket to UK in terms of airline ticket to TW
= $12,000 ÷ $4,000= 3 airline tickets to Taiwan
When a fixed cost is added to each of the long-distance and short-distance goods, 
the relative price of the long-distance decrease (from 5 to 3)
the relative price of short-distance good will increase. (from 0.2 to 0.33)
Accor

tance good will increase. (from 0.2 to 0.33)
According to law of demand, people will then buy relatively more of the long-distance good. 
Tips:
2.9 Relative Price相對價格
例子:
前往台灣的飛機票價是$2,000 & 前往英國的飛機票價是 $10,000
以前往英國機票標示的前往台灣機票的相對價格是
= $2,000 ÷ $10,000= 0.2張前往的英國機票
以前往台灣機票標示的前往英國機票的相對價格是
= $10,000 ÷ $2,000= 5張前往的台灣機票
如果有額外的燃油附加費為 $2,000
以前往英國機票標示的前往台灣機票的相對價格是
= $4,000 ÷ $12,000= 0.33張前往的英國機票
以前往台灣機票標示的前往英國機票的相對價格是
= $12,000 ÷ $4,000= 3張前往的台灣機票
當長途飛航與短途飛航的固定成本增加時, 
長途飛航的相對價格會下降 (從5到3)
短途飛航的相對價格會上升 (從0.2到0.33)
根據需求定律，人們會傾向購買更多長途飛航

2014DSE LQ9c
Many countries have considered imposing ‘soft drink tax’ for health reasons. Suppose a country decides to impose a per unit tax on the sellers for every bottle of soft drink sold in the market. ‘As the amount of tax imposed on each bottle is the same irrespective of the bottle size, the proportion of larger-bottle soft drinks sold in the market will increase.’ Explain this argument using the law of demand.(5)
許多國家以國民健康爲理由考慮徴收「汽水稅」。假設某國決定對賣家在市場中售出的每瓶汽水徵收一項從量稅。「不論瓶子大小，對每瓶汽水均徴收等額的稅款，因此大瓶裝汽水在市場所售出的比例會上升。」以需求定律解釋這論點。     (5)                         
_______________________________________________________________________________________
____________________________

_____________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
Marking Scheme:
Law of demand: when the price of a good increases, the quantity demanded of the good decreases, vice versa, ceteris paribus. Here the unit of measurement to which the tax applies is “bottle” rather than volume (e.g., in litres), of soft drinks. When the same amount of tax is added to each unit (bottle), then – per volume of liquid contained – larger-bottle soft drinks would become cheaper than the small-bottle ones. The relative price of larger-bottle soft drinks (in terms of small-bottle ones) thus drops. The law of demand then implies that people would buy more larger-bottle soft drinks relative to small-bottle ones, so the proportion of larger-bottle soft drinks sold in the market would increase.(5)
參考答案：
需求定律

s sold in the market would increase.(5)
參考答案：
需求定律：當某物品的價格上升，該物品的需求量下跌，反之亦然，其他因素維持不變。計算稅款的單位是「每瓶」，而不是汽水的容量(例如每公升)。當對每單位(每瓶)汽水徵收同額的稅項，以所容納的汽水容量計算，大瓶汽水變得較小瓶汽水便宜。大瓶汽水的相對價格(以小瓶汽水計算)下降。需求定律意味著相對小瓶汽水，人們會購買更多大瓶汽水，所以大瓶汽水的比例會上升。                                                                     (5)

Factors affecting price elasticity of supply 
 Students are expected to calculate the (arc) price elasticity of demand, i.e. the average price and quantity method. 
 Students are expected to explain the relationship among price change, price elasticity and total revenue. 
 Students are expected to explain how the price ranges, availability of substitutes, degree of necessity, time, durability, proportion of income spent on the good and number of uses, etc. affect the price elasticity of demand.
 Students are NOT expected to grasp the second law of demand, but they are expected to understand that price elasticity of demand is affected by the time for adjustment
 Students are expected to calculate the (arc) price elasticity of supply, i.e. the average price and quantity method.
 Students are expected to explain how factor mobility,

dents are expected to explain how factor mobility, flexibility of production and time, etc. affect the price elasticity of supply.

3.3Price Elasticity of Demanded (Ed) 需求彈性
Measure the responsiveness (sensitivity) of quantity demanded to a price change 
(i.e. % change in Qd vs % change in Price)
衡量需求量對價格變化的反應（敏感度）
(即比較需求量的百分比變化與價格的百分比變化)

Perfectly Inelastic demand (完全無彈性需求)i.e.

3.6LQ Demonstration 長題目示範
Change in Price and Total Revenue
Suppose Mastery Tutorial Centre increase its tuition fee. With the aid of a diagram, explain why its total revenue would increase. 
價格和總收入的變化
假設Mastery Tutorial Centre增加了學費。借助圖表，解釋為什麼它的總收入會增加。
When Mastery Tutorial Centre

。借助圖表，解釋為什麼它的總收入會增加。
When Mastery Tutorial Centre increase its tuition fee (P1 to P2)
Quantity demanded of the tutorial service decrease (Q1 to Q2)
Since the demand is inelastic,
Percentage increase in price is greater than percentage decrease in quantity
Total revenue increase (P1 x Q1 to P2 x Q2)
Mastery Tutorial Centre 增加學費時 (P1 to P2)
對補習服務的需求量減少（Q1 to Q2）
假設需求低彈性，
價格上升的百分比大於需求量減少的百分比
總收入增加（P1 x Q1 至 P2 x Q2）
3.6LQ Demonstration 長題目示範
Change in Supply and Total Revenue
Suppose the technology of producing TV improves. With the aid of diagram, explain why the total revenue of TV producer would remain unchanged.
供應和總收入的變化
假設製作電視的技術有所改進。借助圖表，解釋為什麼電視製作的總收入保持不變。
When the technology of producing TV improves,
Production cost of TV decrease
Supply of TV increase (S1 to S2)
Price decrease (P1 to P2)
Quantity increase (Q1 to Q2)
Since the demand is unitary elastic
Percentage decrease in price is equal to percentage increase in quantity
Total revenue remains unchanged (P1 x Q1 to P2 x Q2)
 當製作電視的技術進步時，
 電視生產成本將會下降
 電

(P1 x Q1 to P2 x Q2)
 當製作電視的技術進步時，
 電視生產成本將會下降
 電視供應增加（S1 至 S2）
 價格下降（P1 至 P2）
 交易量增加（Q1 至 Q2）
 由於需求是單一彈性的
 價格下降的百分比等於需求量增加的百分比
總收入保持不變（P1 x Q1 至 P2 x Q2）
Example Long Question 1 (1993 CE Q1a)
‘Yummy Yummy’ is a fast food shop. In a promotion campaign, the shop reduced its selling price of a hamburger from $4.2 to $3.5. Later it was learnt that the total expenditure of customers on hamburgers increased despite the price cut. With the aid of diagram, explain this phenomenon in terms of the price elasticity of demand.                  (7 marks)
“美味美味”是一家快餐店。在一次促銷活動中，這家商店將漢堡包的售價從 $4.2元降到了 $3.5元。後來得知儘管降價，但顧客在漢堡包上的總支出還是增加了。借助圖表，從需求價格彈性的角度解釋這一現象。 （7 分）

ce-cut will reduce our newspaper’s total sales revenue.’

總經理：“降價會減少我們報紙的總銷售收入。”
銷售經理：“即使我們不降價，別人降價了，我們報紙的銷售總收入還是會減少。”
借助兩幅獨立的圖表解釋上述兩位經理的陳述背後的論點。             （11 分）

Example Long Question 6
Different boutiques have different strategies for raising their total revenue. Most of them offer great discounts to customers. However, not all of them enjoy a gain in total revenue. Using the concept of price elasticity, explain with the aid of a diagram, under what condition the boutiques will gain from lowering the price.  (8 marks)
不同的精品店有不同的銷售策略來提高他們的總收入。他們中的大多數都為客戶提供很大的折扣。然而，並非所有廠商都享有總收入的增長。運用價格彈性的概念，借助圖表解釋精品店在什麼情況下會從降價中獲益。 
                                            (8分)

3.8Factors Affecting Elasticity of Demand影響需求彈性的因素
Availability of Substitutes
More consumers consume substitutes when price increases 
(i.e. Higher percentage decrease in quantity)
Ed increase
Elastic: drinks, transport
Inelastic: drugs
替代品的可用性 / 替代品的多少
當價格上漲時，更多的消費者會轉移消費替代品
(需求量增加的百分比較高)
需求彈性增加
彈性需求：飲料、運輸
低彈性需求：藥物
Degree of Necessity
Degree of necessity increase
Hard to reduce consumption
Lower percentage decrease in quantity when price increase 
Ed decrease
Elastic: jewelry, private cars
Inelastic: rice, salt
必要程度
必要性增加
難以降低/減少消費
價格上漲時數量減少百分比較低
需求彈性降低
彈性需求：珠寶、私家車
低彈性需求：大米、鹽
Number of uses
Number of uses increase
Price decrease
Users from different sectors will buy more of it since its number of uses is large 
Higher percentage increase in quantity

is large 
Higher percentage increase in quantity when price decrease
Ed increase
Elastic: timber
Inelastic: Specific screw
貨品的用途
貨品的用途增加
降價會吸引大量買家
不同行業的買家會購買更多 (因為它的用途很多)
價格下降時數量增加百分比更高
需求彈性增加
彈性需求：木材
低彈性需求：特定螺釘

More adjustment time to find substitutes
Higher percentage decrease in quantity when price increase

用於商品的收入比例很小

7.  Item scarcity
Higher scarcity

Es decrease

Chapter 4 _ Market Intervention 市場干預
Price Ceiling 
Price Ceiling is a maximum price set on goods
or services
價格上限
價格上限是對商品或服務設定的最高價格
Price Floor
Price floor is a minimum price set on goods and
services.
價格下限
價格下限是對商品和服務設定的最低價格。
Quota
Quota is a restriction on the total amount of output sales. It fixes the maximum quantity that sellers can offer for sales.
配額 / 限額
配額是對輸出銷售總量的限制。它限制了賣家可以提供的最大銷售數量 / 供應量。
Per-unit Tax
Fixed amount of tax imposed on each unit of
goods and services.
從量稅
從量稅是對每單位商品和服務徵收的從量稅額。
Per-unit Subsidy
Per-unit Subsidy is a fixed amount of subsidy
given on each unit of goods or services.
從量津貼 / 從量補貼
從量補貼是對每單位商品或服務給予的定數額的補
貼。

Price Ceiling Effect:
Protection of consumers’ interests, prevent rising prices of goods and inflation. 
(E.g. transport, rent control)

1.  保護消費者意欲 / 利益，防止物價上漲和通脹。
（例如交通、租金管制）
Excess demand occurs and black market may appear. 
(Black market means buyers buy a product at a price higher than the controlled price.) 
(E.g. houses, tickets)
3.  供不應求 / 超額需求，可能出現黑市。
（黑市是指買家以高於管制價格的價格購買產品。）
（例如房屋、門票）

5.Product quality tends to increase.

4.4Per Unit Tax 從量稅
Per Unit Tax Effect:
Per unit tax increase the production cost, supply o

er unit tax increase the production cost, supply of product will decrease. Equilibrium price will increase and the equilibrium quantity decrease.
從量稅影響：
1.  從量稅增加生產成本，產品供應減少。均衡價格會增
.   加，均衡交易量會減少。
Average quality of products and services will increase.
2.  產品和服務的平均質量將提高。
The part which equilibrium price changes must belongs to consumer’s tax burden, and the remaining part goes to the producer’s tax burden.
3.  均衡價格變動的部分為消費者的稅項負擔，剩下的部
.   分屬於生產者的稅項負擔。

4.5Per Unit Subsidy 從量津貼/從量補貼
Per Unit Subsidy effect:
Help and encourage production by having more capital to use
從量稅津貼
1. 通過使用更多資金進行補貼來幫助和鼓勵生產
Increase consumption 
2. 增加消費
To help the poor (Equity)
3. 扶貧（公平）
Intervene price mechanism: Consumers assigned resource mistakenly, buy those goods which is not willing to buy originally
4. 干預價格機制：消費者錯誤分配資源 (資源錯配)，購
.  買原本不願意購買的商品
Subsidy came from tax: Add further burden to the taxpayer, but producer enjoy additional benefits.
5. 稅收補貼：增加了納稅人的負擔，但生產者享受額外
.  的補貼。
Average quality of products and services will decrease.
6. 產品和服務的平均質量會下降。
The part which leads to the change in equilibrium price must belongs to consumer, and the remaining part belongs to the producer.
7. 導致均衡價格變動的部分為消費者津貼利益，其餘部
.  分屬於生產者津貼利益。

Marking Scheme:
(a)Tobacco duty would increase the market price of duty-paid cigarettes and thus reduce its quantity demanded (1)
If cigarette consumers are addicted, their demand for cigarettes would be inelastic, so the percentage decrease in quantity demanded would be smaller than the percentage increase in price. In this case, even a huge increase in tobacco duty may not be effective in reducing the consumption of cigarettes.(3)
(b)Verbal Explanation:
On the one hand, due to the HK Customs’ crack-down effort, the supply of duty-not-paid cigarettes would drop. On the other hand, as duty-paid cigarettes and duty-not-paid cigarettes are substitutes, tobacco duty woul

paid cigarettes are substitutes, tobacco duty would increase the demand for duty-not-paid cigarettes. It is thus possible that the total quantity of duty-not-paid cigarettes sold would increase if the increase in demand has a bigger effect on the quantity transacted than the decrease in supply. (4)
Indicate on the diagram:
Rightward shift of the demand curve (1)
Leftward shift of the supply curve(1)
Final equilibrium with a higher quantity transacted (1)
(a)煙草稅增加已完稅香煙的市場價格，故令其需求量下降。(1)
若香煙消費者已吸煙成癮，他們對香煙的需求屬低彈性。因此需求量下降的百分率小於價格上升的百分率。在這情況下，即使煙草稅大幅上升，也未能有效減低香煙的消費。(3)
(b)文字解說：
一方面，未完稅香煙的供應因海關的打擊而下降。另一方面，因為未完稅和已完稅香煙屬代替品，煙草稅令未完稅香煙的需求上升。未完稅香煙的銷售量有可能會上升，若需求上升對交易量的影響大於供應下降。(4)

供應曲線向左移(1)
最終均衡點的交易量較高(1)

chunks.text:
Limitations
 Students are expected to 
(i) understand the meaning of primary, secondary and tertiary production,
(ii) give examples of different types of production, 
(iii) classify a given industry into primary, secondary or tertiary production 
(iv) describe their inter-relationship, and
(v) recognise the contributions of each type of production to the Hong Kong economy.
 Students are expected to 
(i) understand that production involves production of 
goods and / or provision of services, 
(ii) understand the meaning of producer and consumer 
goods, and 
(iii) give examples of producer and consumer goods. 
 Students are expected to 
(i) understand the definition of private good,
(ii) understand that public good is defined as a good which is non-rival and non-excludable in consumption, and
(iii) give examples and distinguish whether a given good is private good or public good.
[Note: Impure public good is NOT required.]
 Students are expected to

and the meaning of simple, complex and regional division of labour,

(iv) recognise the limitations of division of labour.

Chapter 5 _ Production & Division of Labor
生產與分工
5.3Classification of Outputs 產出的分類
Production is the process of turning inputs into outputs.
(i.e. Turning resources into goods and services)
生產是將投入轉為產出的過程
(即將資源轉化成物品或服務)
Classified by physical form以物理形態作分類
Goods:
Tangible, Visible 
 E.g. iPhone, books, table, TV programmes… 
物品：
可觸碰的，可看見的
如iPhone、書、檯、電視節目…
Services:
Intangible, Invisible 
E.g. Massage, financial service, transportation, insurance… 
服務：
不可觸碰的，不可看見的
如按摩、金融服務、交通、保險…
Classified by uses以用途作分類
TYPE 1 - Consumer good vs Producer good 消費者物品與生產者物品的比較
Consumer goods
Bought by consumer to satisfy consumer wants directly
Examples: Food 
消費者物品
由消費者購買以直接滿足慾望
例子: 食物 
Producer goods (capital good)
Bought by producer to help production
Example: Raw materials, machine
生產者物品（資本物品）
由生產者購買以協助生產
例子: 原材料、機器 
5.3Classification of Outputs Concept Corner: 
1.In general, goods are tangible and visible T / F
一般而言，物品是可觸碰的和可看見的
∵ 
2.In general, services are intangible and invisible T / F
一般而言，服務是不可觸碰

metadata.source: Ch5 Production and DIvision of Labour NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
es are intangible and invisible T / F
一般而言，服務是不可觸碰的和不可看見的
∵ 
3.Consumer goods are used to satisfy consumers’ want directly T / F
消費者物品是用以直接滿足消費者的慾望
∵ 
4.Producer goods are used to satisfy producers’ want directlyT / F
生產者物品是用以直接滿足生產者的慾望
∵ 
5.Same good can only be either consumer good or producer goodT / F
同一種物品只能是消費者物品或生產者物品
∵ 
6.Apple can only be consumer goods T / F
蘋果只能是消費者物品
∵ 
7.Production is the process of turning inputs into outputs.T / F
生產是將投入轉為產出的過程
∵ 
ANS: TTTTF FT
5.3 Classification of Outputs產出的分類
TYPE 2 - Private goods VS Public goods 私用品與共用品的比較
Private goods
Rival in consumption (i.e. when a person consumes it, the amount available to others will be reduced) 
Not concurrent in consumption (i.e. people cannot consume it at the same time)
Marginal cost increases when serving one more quantity
E.g. Pencil, bag, notebook, bed
私用品
競爭性消費（當一個人消費該物品時，會減少他人可享用該物品的數量）
排他性消費（即人們不能同時消費該物品）
邊際成本隨着服務/提供多一件而上升
例如：鉛筆、袋、筆記本、床
Public goods
Non-rival in consumption (i.e. people can concurrently consume it without

chunks.text:
n (i.e. people can concurrently consume it without reducing the amount available to others)
Concurrent in consumption (i.e. all people can consume it at the same time)
Marginal cost will not increase when serving one more quantity
E.g. Firework, air-con, national defense, knowledge in a textbook
共用品
非競爭性消費（即人們能同時消費該物品而不會減少他人可享用該物品的數量）
非排他性消費（即所有人能同時消費該物品）
邊際成本不會隨着服務 / 提供多一件而上升
例如：煙花、冷氣、國防、教科書內的知識
REMARKS 備註:
Is the marginal cost for serving one more unit PUBLIC GOOD must be zero?
提供多一件共用品的邊際成本是否必然等於零？
Is the marginal cost for producing PUBLIC GOOD must be zero?
生產共用品的邊際成本是否必然等於零？
Is the public good a free-of-charge good?
共用品是否免費的物品？
Is a public good a good provided by the government to the public?
共用品是否由政府提供給大眾的物品？

1.In general, private good is non-rival and non-excludable  T / F
一般而言，私用品是非競爭性和非排他性的
∵

如該物品能被同時消費，它是私用品

3.Cost of serving one more unit public good is larger than zeroT / F
提供多一件

re unit public good is larger than zeroT / F

共用品是免費的

私用品必然是由個人提供的

在公眾地方出現的物品被界定為共用品

5.4Types of Production生產的類型
Primary Production: 
The production involving extraction or direct use of natural resources. 
(E.g. Farming, Mining, Husbandry, Cultivation)
一級生產: 
涉及開採或直接使用自然資源的生產
（如農業、採礦、畜牧業、耕種）
Secondary Production: 
The production involving turning raw material into semi-finished goods or finished goods. 
(E.g. Manufacturing, Construction, Electricity and Water Supply, Reclamation of Land, Spinning and Weaving)
二級生產: 
將原材料變成半成品或成品的生產
（如製造業、建築業、電力及供水、填海造地、紡織業）
Tertiary Production: 
The production involving provision of services. 
(E.g. Education, Transportation, Hot

metadata.source: Ch5 Production and DIvision of Labour NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
of services. 
(E.g. Education, Transportation, Hotel Service, Retailing and Wholesaling, Banking, Traveling, Legal Service, Repair, Delivery, Finance, Insurance, Accounting, Communication.)
三級生產: 
涉及提供服務的生產
（如教育、交通、酒店服務、零售和批發、銀行、旅遊、法律服務、修理、送貨、金融、會計、通訊）
Summary 總結:
Primary Production
一級生產
Extract or Direct use of natural resources in the production processes.
E.g. Fishing, Farming, Mining
在生產過程中開採或直接使用自然資源
如漁業、農業、採礦
Secondary Production
二級生產
Turning raw material into semi-finished or final goods.
E.g. construction, manufacturing, provision of electricity, water, gas
將原材料變成半成品或成品
如建築業、製造業、電力、供水、天然氣供應
Tertiary Production
**Most important section in HK
三級生產
香港最重要的經濟支柱
Provision of services
E.g. Transportation, Financing, Retailing, Musical performance
提供服務
如交通、金融、零售、音樂表演
5.4Type of Production生產的類型
Three types of production are interdependent 三種生產方式互相依存
REMARKS 備註:
Secondary production produces tangible goods; while tertiary production produces intangible services
二級生產產出有形物品；而三級生產產出無形服務
Secondary production turns

metadata.source: Ch5 Production and DIvision of Labour NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
二級生產產出有形物品；而三級生產產出無形服務
Secondary production turns materials into products; while tertiary production provides services only
二級生產將材料轉化成產品；而三級生產只提供服務
It is worth to note as many students may mix up SECONDARY production and TERTIARY production!!
值得注意的是，很多學生可能會混淆二級生產和三級生產
When you buy an iPhone in Fortress (豐澤) ,_____________ production is engaged in the retail shop.
當你在豐澤購買iPhone時，零售業從事_______________ 生產。
When Apple authorized Foxconn (富士康) to make iPhone, ____________ production is engaged in Foxconn’s factory.
當蘋果授權富士康生產iPhone時，富士康的工廠從事__________________ 生產。
5.5Division of labour 分工
Division of labour is the separation of a work process into a number of tasks
分工是將一個工作過程分解成若干個任務
Division of labour = Specialization
分工 = 專門化
There are THREE types of specialization 專業可分為三種:
Simple division of labour
Labor specializes in producing a particular output.
Each person engages in different occupations to specialize in the production of different goods 
Example: Bus driver specializes in driving
i)    簡單分工
勞工專門生產特定的產品
每個人

metadata.source: Ch5 Production and DIvision of Labour NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
specializes in driving
i)    簡單分工
勞工專門生產特定的產品
每個人從事不同的職業，專門生產不同的物品
例子：巴士司機專職駕駛
Complex division of labour
Labour specializes in producing a particular production stage of output
In a role of teamwork
Example: Different players perform different role in
an NBA game / production of iPhone
複雜分工
勞工專門生產特定階段的產出
發揮團隊合作的作用
例子：不同的球員在一場NBA比賽中扮演不同的角色 / 生產iPhone
Regional / International division of labour
A country or region (locality) specializes in the development of a certain industry
Locality: a district, a city, a country, a group of countries, an economy 
Example: China specializes in producing iPhone,
USA specializes in designing iPhone
iii)   區域 / 國際分工
專門發展某一產業的國家或地區（地方）
地方：一個地區、一個城市、一個國家、一組國家、一個經濟體
例子：中國專門生產iPhone，美國專門設計iPhone
5.5Division of labour Concept Corner: 
1.Simple division of labour implies labour specializes in producing a particular output. T / F
簡單分工意味着勞工專門生產特定的產出
∵ 
2.Complex division of labour implies labour specializes in producing a particular production stage of outputT / F
複雜分工意味着勞工專門生產特定階段的產

roduction stage of outputT / F

在明道教育中心，Shel教英文，Tojai教數學，Carson教經濟，這是簡單分工
∵

∵ 
ANS: TTTTT
5.6Advantage of division of labour 分工的好處
1)Increasing average labour productivity 提高平均勞動生產率
(Productivity = Total output / Total working hour (man-hour))
  (生產率 = 總產量 / 總工時（工時）)
Choosing the most suitable person for the job
選擇最合適的人選 / 知人善任
Practice makes perfect
more skillful at a job due to continuous doing in the same task
ii)  熟能生巧
由於不斷持續做同樣的任務，因此在工作中更加熟練
Saving time in learning all the jobs and changing the tasks 
each labor only specializes in doing a single

metadata.source: Ch5 Production and DIvision of Labour NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
sks 
each labor only specializes in doing a single task
iii)  節省學習所有工作和改變任務的時間
每個勞工只專門做單一任務
Stimulus to mechanization
complex work is divided into simple tasks, firm can easily design specialized machine for each task.
iv)  刺激機械化
複雜的工作被分解成簡單的任務，企業便能夠輕鬆地為每項任務設計專門的機器
Save time in moving around job
each labor can stay at the same place to work to decrease the time used to move to another department
v)  節省四處走動的時間
每個勞工都可以留在同一個地方工作以減少用於轉移到另一個部門的時間
2)Making full use of capital goods 充分利用資本物品
Without division of labor, when a worker is working in one stage, the capital goods for other stage are under-used, with division of labor, workers can work in different stage at the time.
如沒有分工的話，當一個工人在某一個階段工作時，其他階段的資本物品沒有得到充分利用；而如有了分工的話，工人可以同時在不同的階段工作。
3)Saving resources 節省資源
Each worker does not need a full set of tools to complete the whole production process.
每個工人不需要全套工具即可完成他自己所屬的整個生產過程。
4)Improving living standard 提高生活水平
More output can be produced. People can consume a larger variety of goods.
可以生產更多的產出，人們可以消費更多種類的物品。

5.7Disadvantage of division of labour分工的壞處
1)Monotonous and boring work 單調乏味的工作
Workers repeat the same work every day, works become monotonous and boring.
工人每天重複同樣的工作，工作變得單調乏味。
2)Greater risk of unemployment 更大的失業風險
Since workers are trained for a particular job, it is difficult for them to find another type of job.
由於工人接受了特定工作的培訓，他們的技能變得單一，較難找到其他類型的工作。 
3)Greater degree of interdependence  更大程度的互相依賴
A delay on one production stage will affect the whole production process.
一個生產階段的延誤將影響整個生產過程。
4)Standardization of product 產品標準化
Mass production result in standardization of product, which limit the choice of customer.
大量生產導致產品標準化，限制了客戶的選擇。
5)Decline in the quality of craftsmanship 工藝質量下降
Fewer people will specialize in hand-made products because of standardization of production. The overall quality of craftsmanship decrease.
由於生產標準化，令更少的人專門從事手工產品，整體工藝質量下降。
6)Limited skills of workers 工人技能有限
With division of la

mited skills of workers 工人技能有限
With division of labor
有分工
Workers are not required to learn all production stage but only one of the production stage.
工人不需要學習所有生產階段的技能，只需學習一個生產階段的技能便足夠。
5.7Disadvantage of division of labour分工的壞處
1)Monotonous and boring work 單調乏味的工作
2)Greater risk of unemployment 更大的失業風險
3)Greater degree of interdependence 更大程度的互相依賴
4)Standardization of product產品標準化
5)Decline in the quality of craftsmanship 工藝質量下降
6)Limited skills of workers 工人技能有限
5.8Limitation of division of labour分工的限制
1)Nature of product  產品的性質
Division of labour results in standardized products, it is not suitable for those industries that requires individuality and unique styles such as painting.
分工導致產品標準化，不適用於繪畫等需要個性化、風格獨特的行業。
2)Size of market 市場的規模
If the market is small, division of labor can only be carried out to a limited extent only.
如果市場很小，分工只能有限度地進行。
3)Possibilities of trade 貿易的可能性
If trade is prohibited, it is not beneficial to practice division of labor.
如果禁止貿易，不利於分工。

下列哪項有關歐洲足球賽事的電視廣播的陳述是正確的？ 
A. 若市民可在公眾場所如餐廳和卡拉OK欣賞賽事廣播，這些賽事的電視廣播便是共用品。 
B. 若某本地電視台購買了在本地的獨家播映權，這些賽事的電視廣播便是私用品。 
C. 這些賽事的電視廣播是免費用品，因為服務額外一位觀眾是沒有成本的。 
D. 若足球訓練學校播放這些比賽作教導兒童之用，這些賽事的電視廣播便是生產者用品。

A. 二級            B. 三級             C. 一級及二級       D. 二級及三級 
Example Question 3 CE1999MCQ13
Mr. Law is a full-time teacher in an aided secondary school in Hong Kong. He is a __________ producer and he is paid using a ________ method.
A. secondary ... time rate B. secondary ... piece rateC. tertiary ... time rate D. tertiary ... piece rate
羅先生是香港某津貼中學的一位全職教師。他是一位_______生產者， 以 ______ 方式支薪。 
A. 二級 …… 「計時」B. 二級 …… 「計件」 C. 三級 …… 「計時」 D. 三級 …… 「計件」 
Example Question 4 CE2002MCQ12
Which of the following statements about production is correct?
A. Developed countries have no primary production.
B. Students selling flags for charity organizations are engaging in production.
C. An Indian restaurant engages in tertiary production only.
D. The car repair work belongs to secondary production.
下列哪一項關於生產的描述是正確的？ 
A. 已發展國家沒有一級生產。 
B. 學生為慈善機構賣旗籌款是一種生產活動。 
C. 一家印度餐廳的生產只涉及三級生產。 
D. 維修車輛屬於

由上表，我們可以推論從1985年至2005年， 
A. 一級及二級生產的就業人數減小了，三級生產的就業人數則增加了。 
B. 整體失業率維持不變。 
C. 三級生產對本地生產總值的貢獻增加了。 
D. 二級生產在就業上的重要性相對地減小了。 
Example Question 7 DSEPPMCQ4
Which of the following descriptions about the relationship between the three types of production is correct?
A. Primary production provides raw materials for the other types of production.
B. Secondary production does not rely on tertiary production to produce finished goods. 
C. Tertiary production relies only on primary production.
D. The three types of production are independent, and tertiary production is the most important.
下列哪項對三類生產之間的關係的描述是正確的？  
A. 一級生產為其他類型的生產提供原材料。  
B. 二級生產毋須依賴三級生產去製造完成品。  
C. 三級生產只依賴一級生產。  
D. 三類生產是獨立的，其中三級生產為最重要。

Example Question 1 CE1992MCQ11
The Ha family and the Wong family are living next door to each other and their children go to the same kindergarten. The children are taken to school by Mr. Ha and brought home by Mr. Wong. This is an example of
A. division of labourB. diseconomies of scaleC. diminishing returnsD. absolute advantage
夏家與黃家為鄰，他們的小孩子都在同一幼稚園就讀。夏先生送他們上 學，黃太接他們放學回家。這是一個_____的例子。 
A. 分工                   B. 規模負經濟 C. 回報遞減             D. 絕對優勢 
Example Question 2 CE1993MCQ15
Specialization is beneficial to an economy because
A. more resources are used in producing capital goodsB. the threat of protectionism can be avoided
C. the risk of unemployment is loweredD. the productivity of the factors of production is raised
專門化對經濟有好處是因為 
A. 可有更多資源使用在生產資本物品上。 B. 可免受保護主義的威脅。 
C. 失業的可能性較低。 D. 生產因素的生產力獲得提高。 
Example Question 3 CE1994MCQ59
Which of the following is NOT an advantage of division of labour? 
A. higher work incentiveB. higher labor productivity
C.

gher work incentiveB. higher labor productivity

下列哪一項不是分工的好處？ 
A. 有更大工作熱誠                              B. 勞動生產力更高 
C. 需要較少量的工具                            D. 合適的人員做合適的工作 
 
Example Question 4 CE1999MCQ52
Which of the following is NOT an advantage of division of labour?
A. greater economies of scaleB. economy in the use of capital
C. higher labour productivityD. improved skill through more practice
下列哪一項並非分工的好處？ 
A. 可享有更大的規模經濟                        B. 更經濟地使用工具 
C. 提高勞工生產力                             D. 透過更多練習使技巧得以改進 
Example Question 5 CE2000MCQ19
When division of labour is practised,
A. workers have greater job satisfaction.
B. machines are used more intensively.
C. producers produce a greater variety of goods to suit different consumer tastes.
D. the occupational mobility of workers increases.
若實施分工， 
A. 工人會有更大的工作滿足感。 
B. 機器的使用率會更高。 
C. 生產商會生產更多種類的貨品以迎合不同顧客的口味。 
D. 工人的職業流動性會增加。 
Example Question 6 CE2001MCQ16
Which of the following

chunks.text:
ple Question 6 CE2001MCQ16
Which of the following is an advantage of division of labour?
A. A wider choice of goods and services can be provided.B. Workers have a greater incentive to work.
C. Workers have higher occupational mobility.D. Less time is required to train workers.
下列哪一項是分工的好處？ 
A. 有更多種類的物品和服務可供選擇。 B. 工人更有工作熱誠。 
C. 工人的職業流動性較高。 D. 訓練工人需時較少。 
Example Question 7 CE2003MCQ17
In a secondary school, different teachers are responsible for teaching different subjects. Which of the following are reasons for the above arrangement?
(1) It can reduce the cost of training.
(2) Practice makes perfect.
(3) Different teachers are good at teaching different subjects.
A. (1) and (2) only B. (1) and (3) only C. (2) and (3) onlyD. (1), (2) and (3)
在一所中學裏，不同的教員負責任教不同的科目。下列哪項是上述安排的原因？ 
（1）減低訓練成本 
（2）熟能生巧 
（3）不同教員擅長任教不同的科目 
A. 只有 (1)，(2) B. 只有 (1)，(3) C. 只有 (2)，(3) D. (1)，(2)，(3)

A. firms will enjoy greater economies of scale.B. workers will have a hi

A. 廠商會享有更多規模經濟。 B. 工人的職業流動性會更高。 
C. 工人會有更多收入。 D. 每工時產出會上升。 
Example Question 9 CE2006MCQ15
When division of labour is practiced, workers will have a ____ occupational mobility because ____.
A. higher ... the labour productivity is raisedB. higher ... the capital goods are used more fully
C. lower ... work becomes dull and monotonousD. lower ... workers are only trained for a particular skill
若實施分工，工人的職業流動性會______，因為_______。 
A. 提高 ...... 勞工生產力獲得提升 B. 提高 ...... 資本工具更能物盡其用 
C. 降低 ...... 工作變得沉悶單調 D. 降低 ...... 工人只獲得某一特定技能的培訓 
Example Question 10 CE2007MCQ14
Division of labour can raise labour productivity. One reason is that
A. fewer tools are needed.
B. geographical mobility is increased
C. time is saved from not moving from task to task
D. there would be a higher degree of interdependence among different producing stages
分工可提高勞工生產力，其中一個原因是 
A. 使用較小工具。

分工可提高勞工生產力，其中一個原因是 
A. 使用較小工具。 
B. 增加地域流動性。 
C. 節省時間，因為工人毋須轉換工序。 
D. 不同工序之間的依賴性較高。 
Example Question 11 DSE2018MCQ11
A professor has decided to change the way of marking the exam papers of his project. Instead of asking his teaching assistants to mark the whole paper for their own classes, he assigns each teaching assistant to mark one question of the paper for the entire candidature. Which of the following situations would likely occur?
(1)The marking will be more accurate since practice makes perfect
(2)Training time will be reduced as each assistant only needs to know part of the marking schemes 
(3)The tools required for marking will be reduced such as fewer red pens are needed
A. (1) and (2) onlyB. (1) and (3) onlyC. (2) and (3) onlyD. (1), (2) and (3)
一位教授決定改變所任教科目的試卷評改方法：他不再要求其助教們批改他們所屬班別的整份試卷，而是指派每位助教批改全體考生整份試卷的其中一題，下列哪些情況可能會出現？ 
(1)評改會更準確，因為熟能生巧。 
(2)訓練的時間會減小，因為每位助教只需要了解評卷參考的一部分 
(3)評改所需的工具會減小，例如需要較少的紅色原字筆 
A. 只有 (1)，(2)       B. 只有 (1)，(3)        C. 只有 (2)，(3)         D. (1)，(2)，(3

To cope with the problem of a rapidly aging population in Hong Kong, the government has proposed a retirement protection scheme under which eligible elderly would receive $38 760 per year. Mr. Chan, a street sleeper, is eligible to receive $38 760 under this scheme. He would be able to afford the service of an elderly care centre and would spend $30 000 of the money to pay the yearly charges. To which type of production does elderly care service belong?(1)
為了應付香港人口急速老化的問題，政府建議一項退休保障計劃，計劃下合資格長者每年可領取$38,760。陳先生是一位露宿者。在該計劃下，他合資格領取$38,760。他便能負擔安老院服務，並會花費其中的$30000支付一年的院費。安老服務屬那一類別的生產？

chunks.text:
/ (Principle of) comparative advantage(1)
Finding the most suitable person for the job: the parents in both families can use their expertises in teaching their children so the children can learn both languages better.
Specialisation and exchange: Mr Wong can specialise teaching English, i.e. the children learning Japanese from Mrs. Chan. The children can learn both languages better with a lower cost. (Similar argument applies to Mrs Chan in her teaching of Japanese as well.) 
Any other relevant point(2)
分工 / 專門化  
指派最適合的人擔任工作：雙方的家長可以運用他們的專長教育小孩，故此小孩能更有效地學習兩種語言。 
生產和交易專門化：王先生能專門教授英文（即從事其機會成本較低的生產）以換取其孩子向陳太太學習日文。兩家的小孩能以較低成來更有效地學習兩種語文。（相似的論點也適用於陳太太教授日文。） 
任何其他相關點  
(b)Public good. It is because the videos are non-rivalrous in consumption as people can concurrently
watch the video without affecting one another.(3)
共用品。因為這些短片屬非競爭性消費。人們可以同一時間觀看短片而不會影響其他人。(3)

(b)No, it is not a public good because it is rivalrous in consumption, i.e. it cannot be used concurrently by more-than-one persons.

ut

Concepts and remarks for answering questions about ‘mobility’:
Always use the concept of OPPORTUNITY COST (O.C.) to explain and think about mobility in DSE LQ first:
Opportunity cost = forgone, when O.C. increases, that means the value of stuff you forgone increase, you tend to

e value of stuff you forgone increase, you tend to remain the status quo; vice versa, when O.C. decreases, that means the value of the stuff you can forgone decrease, you tend to change / move to other environments, ‘只有眼前路，沒有身後身’ as 王家衛 said.
O.C. increases  mobility decreases
O.C. decreases  mobility increases
Example question:
Suppose the government reduce the working incentive transportation subsidies. Explain the effect on labour mobility. (3 marks)
State which TYPE of labour mobility will be affected - occupational / geographical
State the change on labour mobility - increase? / decrease?
Use ‘cost’ to explain - ‘the cost of … increase / decrease.’
回答有關“流動性”問題的概念和備註：:
始終使用機會成本 (O.C.) 的概念來解釋，並在DSE LQ 中首要思考流動性相關概念
機會成本 = 放棄，當 O.C.增加，這意味著你放棄的東西的價值增加，於是你傾向於保持現狀；反之亦然，當 O.C.減少，這意味著你可以放棄的東西的價值減少，這使你傾向於改變 / 移動到其他環境，同時印證了《一代宗師》裡王家衛設計的經典對白：「只有眼前路，沒有身後身。」
O.C. 增加  流動性降低
O.C. 下降  流動性增加

說明勞工流動性的變化 - 增加？ / 減少？
使用“成本”來解釋 - “……增加/減少的成本。”
