16.8Taxation Principle徵稅原則
Adam Smith’s Taxation Principles:
亞當·史密夫的稅收原則
Economy:
The  administrative costs  of the collecting the tax payment should be as small as possible relative to tax revenue
E.g. Employers in Hong Kong are required to inform the government about the salaries and pensions of their employees. This reduces the government’s cost in collecting information and preventing tax evasion.
經濟：
收取稅款及評定稅項的行政費用必須比所得的稅款為少
例如香港的僱主必須向政府通報其僱員的薪金和退休金。這降低了政府收集信息和防止逃稅的成本
Equity / Equality:
Tax payment should be in proportion to the taxpayers’ income 
i.e. Tax rate is  proportional 
E.g. I

rs’ income 
i.e. Tax rate is  proportional 
E.g. Imposition of progressive / regressive tax Violate the principle
Cancellation (abolishment) of progressive / regressive tax
Consistent with the principle
公平：
稅款應按納稅人收入比例計算
即稅率是按比例的
例如：實行累進 / 累退稅
違反公平原則
取消（廢除）累進 / 累退稅
    符合原則
Certainty:
The tax obligation of the taxpayers should be stated with certainty 
i.e. time of payment / the tax amount
E.g. The government  makes advertisement / promotion  to make sure that the public understands the taxes. The Hong Kong Inland Revenue Department has established a website to provide taxpayers with information such as the calculation method of tax, as well as the deadline and modes of tax payment. 
確定：
納稅人的納稅義務應明確說明
即付款時間 / 稅額
例如：政府進行廣告/宣傳以確保公眾了解稅收。香港稅務局設立了一個網站，為納稅人提供稅款計算方法、繳稅期限和繳稅方式等信息。
Convenience:
The  time and method  of the tax payment should be convenient to tax payers
E.g. Taxpayers can pay taxes by phone, post, internet, by person.
方便:
繳交或收取稅項的方法和時間應同時便利納稅人和政府
例如納稅人可以通過電話、郵寄、網上、親身等方式繳稅。
The Taxation Principle in Hon

以通過電話、郵寄、網上、親身等方式繳稅。
The Taxation Principle in Hong Kong香港的課稅原則
Source Principle / The Territorial Source Principle 
Only income derived from Hong Kong is taxable
Carson, a Hong Kong resident, set up a firm in Congo and earned a huge profit. 
Since the profit is not derived from Hong Kong, it is not taxable
來源原則/地域來源原則
只有來自香港的收入才需要納稅
香港居民車神在剛果開辦公司，賺取巨額利潤。由於利潤並非來自香港，因此無需繳稅

Example 2 - Decrease in profit tax rate

例三 - 降低物業稅率

業稅率

Example 4 - Decrease in sales tax rate
When the sales tax rate decreases
Production cost decrease thus supply increases
Price decreases and quantity transacted increase
Sales tax revenue may increase -- IF↑in sales tax revenue due to the increase in quantity transacted >  ↓in sales tax revenue due to decrease in tax rate, sales tax revenue will increase eventually

G increase開支升幅 > T increase稅收升幅

Explanation:
When government increases the income tax
Disposable income decreases
Private consumption expenditure decreases by less than 100% of the decrease in disposable income
Increase in government expenditu

disposable income
Increase in government expenditure > decrease in private consumption expenditure 
Aggregate demand will increase eventually  Thus, it is an expansionary fiscal policy 
解釋：
當政府增加薪俸稅時
可支配收入減少
私人消費支出減少幅度小於可支配收入減少幅度的 100%
政府開支增加 > 私人消費開支減少 
總需求最終會增加  因此，這是一種擴張性的財政政策
Balanced Budget平衡預算:
G increase開支升幅 = T increase稅收升幅 
(e.g. G increase $100, T increase $100, People spend 80% of their disposable income)
（例：G上升100元，T上升100元，人們只花費百分之八十的可支配收入）
Explanation:
When government increases the income tax
Disposable income decreases
Private consumption expenditure decreases by less than 100% of the decrease in disposable income
Increase in government expenditure > decrease in private consumption expenditure 
Aggregate demand will increase eventually  Thus, it is an expansionary fiscal policy 
解釋：
當政府增加薪俸稅時
可支配收入減少
私人消費支出減少幅度小於可支配收入減少幅度的 100%
政府開支增加 > 私人消費開支減少 
總需求最終會增加  因此，這是一種擴張性的財政政策
 
Surplus Budget盈餘預算: 
G increase 開支升幅 < T increase 稅收升幅
(e.g. G increase $120, T increase $150, People spend 60% of their di

120, T increase $150, People spend 60% of their disposable income)
（例：G上升120元，T上升150元，人們只花費百分之六十的可支配收入）
Surplus Budget預算盈餘: 
G increase 開支升幅 < T increase稅收升幅
(e.g. G increase $120, T increase $150, People spend 80% of their disposable income)
（例：G上升120元，T上升150元，人們只花費百分之八十的可支配收入）
Surplus Budget預算盈餘: 
G increase 開支升幅 < T increase 稅收升幅
(e.g. G increase $120, T increase $150, People spend 90% of their disposable income)
（例：G上升120元，T上升150元，人們只花費百分之九十的可支配收入）
Explanation:
When the government increase the income tax
Disposable income decreases
Private consumption expenditure decreases by less than 100% of the decrease in disposable income
However, Increase in government expenditure can be greater than / equal to / less than decrease in private consumption expenditure depend on the % of disposable income spent on consumption 
Aggregate demand may increase / remains unchanged / decrease (uncertain)
解釋：
當政府增加薪俸稅時
可支配收入減少
私人消費支出減少幅度小於可支配收入減少幅度的 100%
然而，政府支出的增加可以大於 / 等於 / 小於私人消費支出的減少，這取決於消費佔可支配收入的百分比
總需求可能增加/保持不變/減少（不確定）

小於私人消費支出的減少，這取決於消費佔可支配收入的百分比
總需求可能增加/保持不變/減少（不確定）

Important Reminder:
Surplus Budget:
As long as only surplus budget can bring a contractionary effect (drop in output), if the government implements a surplus budget, the objective probably aims to pursue a contractionary effect. But be careful that the result may be contractionary, neutral or expansionary

盈餘預算：
只要預算盈餘才能帶來緊縮效應（產出下降），如果政府實施預算盈餘，其目標很可能是為了追求緊縮效應。但要注意結果可能是緊縮的、中性的或擴張的

7(b)
Advantages:
An increase in money supply would lower the interest rate and raise investment, leading to an increase in real output in the short run (given upward-sloping aggregate supply). Given idle resources in the economy, more labour (and/or capital) would be used to produce the bigger output, implying an increase in employment or decrease in unemployment. (Contractionary fiscal policy would not result in an increase in GDP and employment.
Disadvantages:
In the long run (with vertical aggregate supply), an increase in money supply would not create any effect on output and employment. But if the fiscal deficits grow so fast that the money supply has to be increased at a higher rate that of real output, high inflation would even arise. (Contractionary fiscal policy would not result in inflation.)

令人滿意。大部分考生能正確運用經濟概念和理論以找出兩個方案的影響。與去年相類似的題目比較，考生的表現有所進步。以下列出兩部分均出現且值得關注的錯誤：

在提案B方面，考生的表現欠佳。大部分考生只提到轉移支付，結論無關題旨。小部分考生運用書本以外的知識，解釋未來世代可能會受損。
(iii)部：
表現一般。部分考生嘗試分析產出和收入分配以外的經濟變量，當中很多都能夠答對，並附以合宜理由，例如：AD下降令價格水平下降，AD下降令失業人數上升等等。

No. The reduction in the profits-tax rate may induce firms to increase their investment expenditure, so they may end up earning higher profit (tax base). Increase in profits tax revenue due to the increase in profits (tax base) may be greater than the decrease in profit tax revenue due to the reduction in tax rate.
不會。利得稅率下降可能會令廠商的投資支出上升，他們賺取的利潤（稅基）可能上升。由利潤（稅基）上升引致利得稅收入的上升可能大於由稅率下調引致利得稅收入的下降。
(4)
(b)
Equality: The new tax is no longer proportional, meaning that it is inconsistent with the equality principle.
Economy: The new tax system may incur a slightly higher administration cost, rendering it less in line with the economy 
principle.
Certainty: Both the new and old tax systems are equally well-defined and announced publicly, so the new one would be just 
as certain as the old. 
Convenie

ne would be just 
as certain as the old. 
Convenience: There is no change in the method of tax collection, so the change in tax system would have no effect on 
convenience.
公平：新稅不再是按比例徵收，故此不符合公平原則。
經濟：新稅制所涉的行政成本略高，較不符合經濟原則。
確定：新稅制和舊稅制同樣清晰並向公眾公佈，故此和以前無異。
方便：徵收稅項的方法沒有轉變，故此稅制改變沒有影響方便原則。

Candidates’ Performance:
(a)
Satisfactory. Some candidates did not realise that a change in the profits tax rate would affect the firm's incentive to invest 
and thus profits from its investment (tax base) as well. Some argued erroneously that the working incentive of labour would 
be affected. Among those who correctly identified the positive effect of the reduction in profits tax rate on income, some 
failed to specify clearly the condition for the profits tax revenue to increase.
令人滿意。部分考生不明白利得稅稅率的改變會影響廠商的投資意欲和投資利潤（稅基），有些考生誤以為會影響
勞工的工作意欲。在能正確指出減低利得稅稅率對收入的擴張效應的考生中，部分未能清楚寫出令利得稅收增加
的條件。
(b)
Fair. Most candidates were able to name one principle of taxation. But only some candidates made use of the information 
provided in the

ates made use of the information 
provided in the question to discuss whether the new tax system was consistent with their chosen principle. Some 
misunderstanding regarding the principles was evident. This included the misinterpretation of the concepts of ‘equity' as a 
requirement for tax payers with bigger profits to be charged at higher tax rates and; *economy' as a reduction in the firm's 
profits tax payments due to a lower tax rate (8.25% instead of 16.5%) on the first $2 million of earned profits (the first tier).
一般。大部分考生能寫出一項稅務原則的名稱，但只有部分考生能運用所提供的資料討論新稅制是否與他們所述
的原則相符。部分考生對稅務原則有以下誤解：
誤將「公平」原則詮釋為賺取較多利潤的納稅人應付更高稅率。
誤將「經濟」原則詮釋為由於首二百萬利潤的（第一階的）稅率較低（8.25%而非16.5%），故廠商能節省所納稅款。

Chapter 17 _ Money & Banking
1. Liquidity 
Liquidity states that the property of an asset to be converted into money or other assets without much lose in value
流動性
流動性指將一樣資產轉化成金錢或其他資產而同時不損失太多價值
17.3Barter Economy (以物易物)
Before the existence of money, barter is the only one way to make transaction possible
Barter is a situation that people exchange goods directly
E.g.
Barry wants an apple whereas Wilson wants a nuclear weapon. Given that Barry is a weapon producers and Wilson is an apple seller. They may exchange the goods they want directly. (Fulfill the double coincidence of wants)
Angie wants a pencil from Ken while Ken does not want the pencil case from Angie. They canno

es not want the pencil case from Angie. They cannot exchange the goods they want directly. (Lack of double coincidence of wants)
Barter Economy Features:
Exist in Money Economy, Non-money Economy
Even in Hong Kong (Money Economy), some of them may still transact the good directly without using money. 
E.g. (Baby Kingdom, Stanley Market, Lihkg)
Not exist in one-man economy
One-man economy is no need for trade
No barter / money will be existed 
Volume of transaction is few
Difficult to find the suitable buyer
Division of labor is few
Difficult to make transaction
People need to make most of the good that they want
Interest may still exist
Buy three pens by trading 3 apples
Or trading 5 apples a week later 
2 more apples are the interest 
在貨幣出現之前，以物易物是使進行交易的唯一方式
以物易物是人們直接交換物品的情況
例如：
Barry想要一個蘋果，而Wilson想要一個核武器。 已知 Barry 是核武器生產商，而 Wilson 是蘋果銷售商。他們可以直接交換他們想要的商品。（滿足雙向需求偶合）
Angie 想要 Ken 的鉛筆，而 Ken 不想要 Angie 的鉛筆盒。他們未能直接交換他們想要的商品。（缺乏雙向需求偶合）

Lack of Double
coincidence of wants

vided

17.4以物易物的問題
缺乏雙向需求偶合

（E.g. 如果習主席不想要核武器，交易是不可能的）

交換的物品可能有很多交換比率

（E.g. 1個蘋果的價值可能等於2隻香蕉、3支紅筆、7張紙）
儲存財富困難

某些物品是會腐壞的 / 有時限的
儲存財富的成本高昂

(E.g. 生果會在短時間內腐爛)
不可分割
某些物品不能被分割至更小單位以作不同大小的交易 / 它們的價值會在分割後大幅下跌
(E.g. 一個蘋果不能被分割成較小單位)
異質
物品的種類未必是同質的，因此很難訂立一個標準交換比率

不方便
某些物品攜帶起來很重
(E.g. 金、鐵、石)

17.7Properties of money
Generally acceptable
Money should be generally accepted by people for settling payment

generally accepted by people for settling payment
(E.g. $20 banknote vs a durian)
Scarce
Money should be scarce in order to maintain its purchasing power, if the supply can be easily changed, its value is fluctuated
(E.g. $20 banknote vs the sand in desert)
Durable
Money should be durable to reduce damage and depreciation
(E.g. $20 banknote vs ice)
Portable
Money should be portable and convenience for carrying the money around for transaction. So that the cost can be reduced.
(E.g. $20 banknote vs metal / gold)
Divisible
Money should be divisible into different denomination without lose in total value so that it can settle payment in different amounts
(E.g. silver vs a chair)
Homogeneous
In general, money of the same value is homogenous in quality to avoid disputes / argue / bargain over exchange ratios. Same value of money usually has the same outlook or uniform characteristic. 
(E.g. $20 banknote vs rock)
17.7貨幣的特徵
普遍接受
貨幣應該要被人們普遍接受用於結算
（例：$20銀行鈔票與榴槤。）
稀少
貨幣應該要是稀少的，以維持其購買力，如果供應可以輕易改變，其價值就會波動
（例：$20銀行鈔票與在沙漠的

應該要是稀少的，以維持其購買力，如果供應可以輕易改變，其價值就會波動
（例：$20銀行鈔票與在沙漠的沙）
耐用
貨幣應該要是耐用的，以減少損壞和折舊
（例：$20銀行鈔票與冰）
便攜的
貨幣應該要是便攜的，方便攜帶貨幣進行交易。這樣就可以降低成本。
（例：$20銀行鈔票與金屬 / 黃金）
可分割
貨幣應該要在不損失總價值的情況下分割成不同面額 / 面值，以便可以進行不同金額的支付
（例：銀與椅子）
同質
一般來說，相同價值的貨幣在質量上應該要是同質的，以避免在兌換比例上發生糾紛 / 爭論 / 討價還價。相同價值的貨幣通常具有相同的外觀或統一的特徵。
（例：$20銀行鈔票與石頭）

2017DSE LQ5
Due to political instability, the currency of a country depreciates sharply and becomes highly volatile. Which TWO functions of money may be weakened? Explain.(4)
由於政局不穩，某國的貨幣大幅貶值，並變得非常波動。這可能削弱了貨幣哪兩項功能？試加以解釋。  (4)

_______________________________________________________________________________________

______________

___________________________________

Marking Scheme:
Functions of money:
Store of value: As the value of the local currency drops in terms of foreign currency, its purchasing power drops in terms of the quantity of foreign goods and services bought.
Standard of deferred payment: As the value of the currency becomes highly volatile, the cost of agreeing on the contracts of future payments of local currency increases.
Medium of exchange: Some multinational firms may refuse to accept the currency if the value fluctuates too much.
貨幣的功能:
價值的儲藏: 由於本地貨幣的價值(以外幣計算)下跌，其購買力(以外國的物品和服務數量計算)會下降。
延期支付的標準: 由於貨幣的價值大幅波動，擬訂日後以本地貨幣支付的合約的成本會上升。
交易的媒介: 若貨幣價值波動太厲害，一些跨國的廠商可能拒收該國貨幣。
(@2)
(@2)
Candidates’ Performance:
Good. Many candidates know that unstable and depreciating currencies weaken their functions as a store of value and a standard for deferred paymen

Marking Scheme:
Unit of account: The price of the hotel accommodation service is expressed in terms of RMB.
Medium of exchange: RMB serves as a medium to facilitate people’s buying and selling of services in the website 
記帳單位：飯店住宿服務的價格以人民幣表示。
交易媒介：人民幣作為媒介，方便人們在網站上買賣服務
(@2)

Candidates’ Performance:
Good. Most candidates were able to identify the relevant functions of money performed by RMB in this context. However, only some of these candidates used the information provided to illustrate their answers. A small number of candidates might have misread the question and chose to discuss the functions of prices (rather than money) in their answers. 
良好。小部分考生能寫出在這情況下人民幣所發揮有關的貨幣功能。但是只有部分考生運用所提供的資料解說他們的答案。少數考生可能誤解題目，作答時選擇討論價格（而非貨幣）的功能。
2020DSE LQ5b
Peter owes Mary $100 and he has no cash in his wallet. He suggests repaying Mary by a cash coupon (with a face value of $100) issued by

cash coupon (with a face value of $100) issued by a supermarket chain. Give TWO economic reasons to explain why Mary would refuse to accept the cash coupon as a repayment of Peter’s debt.(4)
彼德欠瑪莉$100，而他的錢包內並無現金。他向瑪莉提議用某連鎖超市發出的現金券（面值$100）還債。舉出兩個經濟理由解釋為什麼瑪莉會拒絕接受該現金券作為彼德的還債。         (4)
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
_______________________________________________________________________________________
Marking Scheme:
Not generally accepted as the coupon can only be used in the supermarket chain but not other shops.
Not divisible as the coupon is difficult to be broken down into smaller units to facilitate exchange.
Not a good store of value as the supermarket chain may close down and the coupo

the supermarket chain may close down and the coupon become worthless.
Not durable as banknotes because the coupon is not waterproof and may be easily damaged.
Not a legal tender as other people may refuse to accept the coupon as payment.
並非普遍地被接受為交易媒介，因為現金券只能在該連鎖超市使用，其他店舖並不能使用。
並非可分割，因為現金券很難兌換成較細金額以幫助交易較便宜的物品。
並非良好價值儲藏，因為當該連鎖超市關店，現金券價值歸零。
不及紙幣耐用，因為該現金券並不防水，容易損壞。
不是法定貨幣，因為其他人可能拒絕接受現金券作支付之用。                   
(@2)
(@2)
Candidates’ Performance:
Satisfactory. The majority of candidates were able to compare cash and the supermarket coupon from the perspective of ‘function of money’ or ‘features of good money’. A common problem was inaccuracy or imprecision in using such terminology as general acceptability, divisibility, durability medium of exchange, and store of value, resulting in vague or inconsistent answers. 
令人滿意，大部分考生能從「貨幣的功能」或「良好貨幣的特徵」的角度比較現金和超市現金券。考生常見的毛病是未能準確地運用相關術語，例如普遍接受性、可分割、耐用、交易媒介及價值儲藏，以致答案含糊不清或前後不一。

若陳先生可選擇以英鎊(GBP)或美元(USD)收取其薪金，你會建議他選擇哪種貨幣？解釋你的答案。   (4)

受外，以兩個理由解釋什麼香煙在社會中並不是一種良好的貨幣形式。(4)

Marking Scheme:
(a)
USD, because the exchange rate of USD to HKD, thus the purchasing power of USD in Hong Kong, is more stable under the linked exchange rate. In other words, USD involves lower exchange-rate risk for Mr Chan. 
Or
GBP, because GBP is the legal tender in the UK, so he can use GBP directly to buy goods and services during his stay in the UK.
美元，因為美元兌港元因聯繫匯率的緣故會較穩定，故此在香港的購買力也較穩定。換句話說，陳先生選擇美元的外匯風險較低。
或
英鎊，因為英鎊在英國是法定貨幣，故此他在留英期間可以直接用英鎊購買物品和服務。

Not durable, as cigarettes can be easily damaged and cannot be stored for a long time.

for high-value of transactions.

香煙並不耐用，因為香煙容易損壞，不可長時間儲藏。
香煙並非同質，因為香煙物理特徵不一致。

Candidates’ Performance:
(a)
Good. Many candidates managed to apply their knowledge about money and foreign exchange to justify their choices with proper economic reasons. A few candidates picked GBP based on the erroneous argument that, by such choice of currency denomination, Mr Chan would be able to earn more income when converted to HKD because GBP has a higher per-unit value than USD in terms of HKD. A minor few carelessly put down 1 HKD = 7.8 USD under the linked exchange rate. 
(b)
Good. The following is a list of some common errors.
Listening the functions of money (e.g. store of value) or a characteristics of good money (e.g. durable, homogeneous) without relating their answers to the case of cigarettes. 
Ignoring the ‘apart from’ clause of the question and mis-stating

part from’ clause of the question and mis-stating ‘not being generally acceptable’ as their answers. 
Not quite accurate elaboration on the ‘non-divisible’ property of cigarettes.
Misunderstanding that cigarettes are not a good form of money because they are not scarce in the society. 
(a) 
良好。很多考生能運用有關貨幣和外匯的知識，並以確當的經濟理由論證其選擇。少數考生選擇英鎊的理由是錯誤的，他們以為選英鎊可以令陳先生的收入在兌換成港元後，能賺得更多，原因是一英鎊兑港幣的價值較美元為高。有很少數的考生不小心寫出在聯繫匯率下1HKD = 7.8USD。
(b)
良好。下列是一些常犯的錯誤：
-只列出貨幣的功能（如價值儲藏——或良好貨幣的特徵（如耐用、同質）、卻沒有將答案和香煙這個案連繫起來。
-忽略題目中「除了」這字眼，並錯誤以「不獲普遍接受」作為答案。
-對香煙的「很難分割」屬性的解說欠準確。

chunks.text:
Credit creation / contraction and the banking multiplier 
 
 Students are expected to grasp the definitions of M1, M2 and M3 in Hong Kong
 Students are expected to
(i) understand and calculate
- required and actual reserve ratio,
- monetary base,
- maximum and actual banking multiplier, and
- maximum and actual change in money supply, deposits and loans, etc.,
(ii) grasp the assumptions made in calculating the maximum changes in money supply, deposits and loans
etc., and
(iii) describe the process of credit creation / contraction.
[Note: Students are expected to understand that a change in monetary base will affect money supply, deposits and loans, etc.] 
 Starting from S4 in 2013/14, i.e., 2016 HKDSE Examination, students are NOT expected to manipulate cash-deposit ratio in the calculation of credit creation / contraction. 
Chapter 18 _ Money Supply & Deposit Creation
Definition List
1.Credit creation process
-When people deposit money into the banking system / Required reserve ratio decreases
-There will b

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
m / Required reserve ratio decreases
-There will be excess reserves in the banking system 
-The banks will lend out the excess reserve 
-The loan lend out will be re-deposited into the banking system 
-The process will go on and on, until there is no excess reserve
2.Credit contraction process
-When people withdraw money from the banking system / Required reserve ratio increase 
-There will be reserves shortage in the banking system 
-The banks will call back bank loans / sell assets to the public 
-The lender will withdraw deposits to repay bank loan 
-The process will go on and on, until there is no reserve shortage
定義列表
1. 存款創造過程
當人們將錢存入銀行系統時 / 法定儲備比率下降
銀行系統將出現超額儲備
銀行將借出超額儲備
借出的貸款將重新存入銀行系統
這個過程會一直持續下去，直到沒有超額儲備為止
2. 存款收縮過程
當人們從銀行系統提款時 / 法定儲備比率增加
銀行系統將出現儲備短缺
銀行將召回銀行貸款 / 向公眾出售資產
貸方將提取存款以償還銀行貸款
這個過程會一直持續下去，直到沒有儲備短缺

18.3 Money Supply Concept Corner: 
1.Cash held by banks is included in M1     公眾持有現金包括在M1        T / F

∵
3.All kinds of deposit in licensed banks are included in both M1 and M2    持牌銀行中的所有類型存款包括在M1及M2                  T / F
∵
4.Any deposits from restricted licence banks and deposit taking companies are only included in M3    有限制牌照銀行及接受存款公司中的任何存款只包括在M3     T / F

uded in M3    有限制牌照銀行及接受存款公司中的任何存款只包括在M3     T / F

∵
6.Any transaction that remit oversea must lead to a drop in M1    任何匯出海外的交易必定導致M1下跌                T / F
∵
7.Money flow from oversea (HKD) must lead to an increase in M3    從海外匯入的交易（港幣）必定導致M3上升                   T / F

8.NCD issued by both licensed banks and restricted licence banks are included in M3    持牌銀行及有限制牌照銀行發行的可轉讓存款證皆包括在M3                     T / F

18.4About Deposit Creation
Since the cost for printing money is very large, thus, money creation exists.
There are 6 items in the process of money creation.
Actual Reserve:
The exact amount of cash reserve that the bank is holding at this moment
Required Reserve:
The amount that the government require the bank to hold 
Excess Reserve:
The am

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
t require the bank to hold 
Excess Reserve:
The amount that the bank is holding more than the amount that the government requires to hold
= Actual Reserve – Required Reserve
Required Reserve Ratio:
The % that the government requires the bank to have a proportion of asset against liability 
The ratio that the banking system required to hold the real money in order to lend out loan
Loan:
The money that banking systems lend out to the people
Deposit:
The money that owned by public and decided deposit in the banking system
*** $ / MILLION / BILLION etc…
18.4關於存款創造
印鈔所需成本高昂，因此存在貨幣創造
貨幣創造過程有6項部份
1. 實際儲備： 
 此時銀行持有的現金儲備的實際金額  
2. 法定儲備： 
 政府要求銀行最少持有的儲備金額  
3. 超額儲備： 
 銀行現時實際持有的儲備超出政府要求銀行最少持有的儲備金額 
 = 實際儲備 – 法定儲備  
4. 法定儲備比率： 
 政府要求銀行資產兌負債比例的百分比 
 銀行系統為貸款而需要持有貨幣的比率  
5. 貸款： 
 銀行系統借出的金錢  
6. 存款： 
 由公眾持有並決定存放在銀行系統中的貨幣 *** $ / 百萬 / 十億等等…
18.5Formula of Deposit Creation
Actual Reserve Ratio:
Actual Reserve ÷ Deposit 
Required Reserve Ratio:
Required Reserve ÷ Deposit
Required Reserve Ratio = Minimum Required Reserve Ratio =

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
d Reserve Ratio = Minimum Required Reserve Ratio = Minimum Reserve Ratio = Minimum Cash Reserve Ratio
Required Reserve:
Deposit x Required Reserve Ratio
Excess Reserve:
Actual Reserve – Required Reserves
Maximum Banking Multiplier:
1 ÷ Required Reserve Ratio 
Actual Banking Multiplier:
1 ÷ Actual Reserve Ratio
*** For point 5 and 6…
Questions may ask for factors increase or decrease the actual banking multiplier. No need to use the mathematical method or formula but the three assumptions. 
18.5存款創造公式
實際儲備比率：
實際儲備 ÷ 存款
法定儲備比率：
法定儲備 ÷ 存款
法定儲備比率 = 最低法定儲備比率 = 最低儲備比率 = 最低現金儲備比率
法定儲備：
存款 x 法定儲備比率
超額儲備：
實際儲備 – 法定儲備
最大銀行乘數：
1 ÷ 法定儲備比率
實際銀行乘數：
1 ÷ 實際儲備比率
*** 就第5，6點…
題目可能問實際銀行乘數上升或下跌的因素，除了3個前設，不用提供數學計算或公式
18.4 & 18.5 More About the Deposit Creation Concept Corner: 
1.Actual reserve ratio must be larger than or equal to required reserve ratio     實際儲備比率必定大於或等於法定儲備比率                   T / F
∵
2.Required reserve ratio may be larger than actual reserve ratio     法定儲備比率可能大於實際儲備比率                            T / F
∵
3.Excess

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
際儲備比率                            T / F
∵
3.Excess reserve may be larger than actual reserve
    超額儲備可能大於實際儲備               T / F
∵
4.Actual reserve is always equal to required reserve     實際儲備總是等於法定儲備                  T / F
∵
5.Reserve + loan = Deposit
     儲備＋貸款＝存款     T / F
∵
6.Loan = 0 when the required reserve ratio is 100%     當法定儲備比率為100%時，貸款 ＝ 0          T / F
∵
7.Reserve = deposit when the required reserve ratio is 0%     當法定儲備比率為0%時，儲備 ＝ 存款            T / F
∵
8.Excess reserve = Actual reserve – Required reserve      超額儲備 ＝ 實際儲備 – 法定儲備               T / F
∵
9.Maximum banking multiplier = 1 / actual reserve ratio     最大銀行乘數 ＝ 1 / 實際儲備比率                          T / F
∵
10.Loan can be larger than deposit
     貸款可以大於存款            T / F
∵
ANS: TFFFT TFTFF

1. Bank operates on a Fractional Reserve System 
All banks keep a required fraction of their total deposit as required reserves 
 the necessary condition for deposit creation
2. No Excess Reserves 
All banks only keep required reserves and lend out all excess reserves 
3. No Cash Leakage 
All money lend out by banks will be re-dep

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
eakage 
All money lend out by banks will be re-deposited into the banking system 
4. Sufficient Demand for Bank Loans 
Banks can lend out all moneys as much as they want 
(No.2 - 4 are the assumptions to maximize the effect of deposit creation)
1. 銀行實行部份儲備制度
所有銀行保留部份存款作法定儲備
 存款創造的必要條件
2. 無超額儲備
所有銀行只保留法定儲備並借出所有超額儲備
3. 無現金流失
所有借出款項會被重新存入銀行系統
4. 對銀行貸款具足夠需求
銀行可按自身意願借出所有金錢
（No.2 - 4為存款創最大化的前設）
18.6 Process of Deposit Creation & Assumptions Concept Corner:
存款創造過程&前設：
1.There are assumptions in order to maximize the deposit creation     存款創造最大化是存在前設的                            T / F
∵
2.If there is cash leakage, the deposit creation must not be maximized      假設有現金流失，存款創造必定不能最大化                T / F
∵
3.If the bank holds excess reserve, we will consider this case as cash leakage     假設銀行持有超額儲備，我們會視之為現金流失                         T / F
∵
4.Holding no excess reserve must lead to maximize deposit creation     不持有超額儲備必定導致存款創造最大化                         T / F
∵
5.Cash leakage implies the public does not re-deposit all the

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
age implies the public does not re-deposit all the money back to the banking system      現金流失意味著公眾不將所有金錢重新存入銀行系統                              T / F
∵
6.If there is insufficient demand for bank loan, deposit creation may still be maximized     假設對銀行貸款沒有足夠需求，存款創造仍有可能最大化                         T / F
∵
7.Bank operates on a Fractional Reserve System is one of the assumptions     銀行實行部份儲備制度是其中一項前設                         T / F
∵
8.Great demand for bank loan will lead to maximize deposit creation     對銀行貸款需求巨大能夠導致存款創造最大化                 T / F
∵
ANS: TTFFT FFF

DSE Trap:
Be careful to the money supply 
Money supply = cash held by public + deposit 
People may deposit money from their own wallet
Withdraw (decrease in deposit & increase in cash held by public)
Be careful to the key wordings “change in xxx” or “maximum amount of xxx”
Remember the unit shown in the T-account (Million / Billion)
Remember the dollar sign $ 
Be careful to the preposition “to” and “by”
留意貨幣供應
貨幣供應 ＝ 公眾持有現金 ＋ 存款
人們可能從他們自己銀包中取錢存款
提款 (存款減少 & 公眾持有現金增加)
留意字眼「XXX的改變」或「最大可能值」
留意T-account顯示的單位（百萬 / 十億）
留意dollar sign $ 
留意字眼「提高」「提高至」/「降低」「降低至」

18.11 Multiple Choice Question Flow MC框架

18.12Long Question Flow長題目框架
Type 1: Process of deposit creation / contraction
存款創造/收縮的過程
1.Credit creation process
When people deposit money into the banking system / Required reserve ratio decreases
There will be excess reserves in the banking system 
The banks will lend out the excess reserve 
The loan lend out will be re-deposited into the banking system 
The process will go on and on, until there is no excess reserves 
2.Credit contraction process
When people withdraw money from the banking system / Required reserve ratio increase

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
banking system / Required reserve ratio increase 
There will be reserves shortage in the banking system 
The banks will call back bank loans / sell assets to the public 
The borrower will withdraw deposits to repay bank loan 
The process will go on and on, until there is no reserve shortage
1. 存款創造過程 
人們存款入銀行系統 / 法定儲備比率下降 
銀行系統將出現超額儲備 
銀行將借出超額儲備 
借出的貸款將重新存入銀行系統 
這個過程會一直持續下去，直到沒有超額儲備為止  
2. 存款收縮過程 
人們從銀行系統提款 / 法定儲備比率增加 
銀行系統將出現儲備短缺 
銀行將召回銀行貸款 / 向公眾出售資產 
貸方將提取存款以償還銀行貸款 
這個過程會一直持續下去，直到沒有儲備短缺
Type 2: Assumption in deposit creation
存款創造的前設
Bear in mind and recite 
Banks hold no excess reserve / Sufficient demand for bank loans / No cash leakage
***Why actual increase in D  <  maximum increase in D? 
銀行不持有超額儲備/ 對銀行貸款具足夠需求/ 無現金流失
***為何存款實際升幅  < 存款最大升幅
Type 3: Why money supply often a few times more than monetary base?
為何貨幣供應通常比貨幣基礎多幾倍？
Fractional reserves banking system enables the banks to lend out money and create loans, which eventually will be deposited back into the banks as deposits, resulting in a multiple i

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
o the banks as deposits, resulting in a multiple increase in deposits / the banking multiplier is larger than unity.
部分儲備制度使銀行能夠借出資金並創造貸款，最終將作為存款再度存入銀行，導致存款成倍數增加 / 銀行乘數大於1
Type 4: Deposit creation
存款創造
1.Required reserve ratio 法定儲備比率
2.Reserve 儲備
3.Deposit 存款
4.Loan (if needed) 貸款（如需）
5.Money Supply (if needed) 貨幣供應（如需）
6.Change in money supply (if needed) 貨幣供應改變（如需）
***Unit? 單位？？
NOTES
NOTES
NOTES

is a component of Hong Kong’s money supply M2?
A. savings deposits with licensed banks
B. time deposits with deposit-taking companies
C. negotiable certificates of deposit held by banks
D. one-thousand-dollar gold coins issued by the Hong Kong government and held by banks

A. 存於持牌銀行的儲蓄存款B. 存於接受存款公司的定期存款
C. 銀行所持有的可轉讓存款證D. 銀行所持有的由港府發行的千元面額金幣
Example Question 4 CE1992MCQ32
In Hong Kong, savings deposits with licensed banks are 
A. legal tenderB. included in the money supply M1
C. included in the money supply M2D. included in the money supply M2, but excluded from the money supply M3
在香港，存於持牌銀行的儲蓄戶口存款是
A. 法定貨幣B. 包括在貨幣供應定義一(M1)內。
C. 包括在貨幣供應定義二(M2)內。D. 包括在貨幣供應定義二(M2)內，但不包括在貨幣供應定義三(M3)內。
Example Question 5 CE1994MCQ43
If an individual receives a cheque for $1000 from his friend and deposits it in his savings account, Hong Kong’s money supply M1 will _______ and M2 will ________.
A. increase; increase B. remain unchanged; increase
C. decrease; remain unchanged D. remain unchanged; remain unchan

若某君在收到他的朋友簽付的 $1000支票後存入他的儲蓄戶口，香港的貨幣供應定義一會_______而貨幣供應定義二會_______。
A. 上升；上升B. 不變；上升
C. 下降；不變      D. 不變；不變
Example Question 6 CE1995MCQ43
Mr. Chan withdraws $600 000 from his savings deposit with a commercial bank and then deposits $500 000 with a restricted licence bank. The remaining $100 000 is kept at home. The immediate effect is that
A. M1 will increase by $100 000 B. M2 will decrease by $600 000
C. M2 will not change D. M3 will increase by $500 000
陳先生從一商業銀行的儲蓄戶口提取$600 000，然後將$500 000 存於一有限制牌照銀行的戶口內，餘下的$100 000則留在家中。上述事件的即時變化是
A. 貨幣供應定義一(M1)會增加$100 000B. 貨幣供應定義二(M2)會減少$600 000
C. 貨幣供應定義二(M2)維持不變D. 貨幣供應定義三(M3)會增加$500 000 
Example Question 7 CE1996MCQ37
Mr. Chan paid $200000 in cash to buy from Mr. Wong a certificate of deposit issued by a deposit-taking company. Mr. Wong then deposited this $200000 into his savings account. The immediate effects are
(1) M1 decrease by $200000
(2) M2 remains unchanged
(3) M3 increase by $200000
A. (1) and (2) only B.

A. (1) and (2) only B. (1) and (3) only  C. (2) and (3) only    D. (1),(2) and (3)
陳先生以現金20萬元向王先生購買由某接受存款公司發行的存款證。王先生再將這20萬元存入他的儲蓄戶口中。即時變化是：
(1) 貨幣供應定義一會減少20萬元。
(2) 貨幣供應定義二維持不變。
(3) 貨幣供應定義三增加20萬元。
A. 只有(1)、(2)B. 只有(1)、(3)  C. 只有(2)、(3)     D. (1)、(2)及(3)
Example Question 8 CE1997MCQ36
Which of the following will increase M1 in Hong Kong?
A. a repayment of HK$5000 by cheque on a bank loan in Hong Kong
B. the lending of HK$5000 in cash by a bank to a citizen in Hong Kong
C. a new deposit of HK$5000 in a savings account with a bank in Hong Kong
D. a withdrawal of HK$5000 in cash from a current account with a bank in Hong Kong
下列哪一項會增加香港貨幣供應定義一？
A. 某人利用支票償還香港一家銀行港幣$5000的貸款。
B. 某銀行貸款港幣$5000現金予香港一位市民。
C. 香港某銀行收到一項新的儲蓄存款，為數港幣$5000。
D. 香港某銀行一位客戶的往來存款戶口被提取港幣$5000現金。

Example Question 10 CE1998MCQ36
Which of the following will cause an immediate change in the money supply M1 of Hong Kong?
A. You receive $500 cash from your father and deposit it in your current account.
B. You pay $4 000 cash to the landlord, who puts the money in his safe.
C. A fire burnt most of the banknotes kept in a bank.
D. You buy $10 000 worth of furniture with a cheque.

以下哪一項導致本港貨幣供應定義一即時有所改變？
A. 你將父親給予你的$500現金存入往來戶口內。
B. 你支付屋主$4000現金，他存入夾萬中。
C. 一場大火燒毀一間銀行內大部分紙幣。
D. 你用支票買入價值$10 000的傢俬，店主將支票存入他的定期存款戶口內。
Example Question 11 CE1999MCQ39
Suppose in a bank in Hong Kong, Mary withdraws HK$3 000 from her savings account and changes it for US dollars in cash. Which of the following statements about the HKD money supply is correct?
A. HK$ M1 will increase while HK$ M2 will remain unchanged.
B. HK$ M1 will remain unchanged while HK$ M3 will decrease.
C. Both HK$ M1 and HK$ M3 will remain unchanged.
D. Both HK$ M1 and HK$ M2 will decrease.
假設在香港某銀行裡，紅玉從儲蓄戶口中提取港幣3000元，並用它換取了美元現金。下列哪一項關於港元貨幣供應的描述是正確的？
A. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）會上升，而港元貨幣供應（定義二）會維持不變。
B. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）會維持不變，而港元貨幣供應（定義三）會下跌。
C. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）及（定義三）均維持不變。
D. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）及（定義二）均會下跌。
Example Question 12 CE2000MCQ27
Mr. Wong withdraws $500 000 in cash from a deposit-taking company. He then deposits $300 000 with a bank as time deposit and r

A. Both M1 and M3 will increase. B. Both M1 and M3 will decrease.
C. M1 will remain unchanged and M3 will decrease. D. Both M1 and M3 will remain unchanged.
王先生在某接受存款公司提取$500 000現金。他將$300 000存放於某銀行做定期存款，又將其餘的$200 000匯於海外來還債。以下哪一項關於貨幣供應定義一（M1）和貨幣供應定義三 （M3） 的描述是正確的？
A. M1和M3兩者都會上升。B. M1和M3兩者都會下降。
C. M1會維持不變而M3會下降。D. M1和M3兩者都會維持不變。

Example Question 15 CE2003MCQ32
Mr. Chan withdraws HK$50000 from his savings account with a bank in Hong Kong and remits HK$40000 through a bank to the UK. He changes the remaining HK$10000 into the newly issued HK$10 notes and puts them into safety deposit box in a bank. Which of the following would be the immediate effect the above actions on t

chunks.text:
uld be the immediate effect the above actions on the Hong Kong dollar money supply? 
A. Both the HK$ M2 and M3 will decrease by HK$40000B. HK$ M1 will increase by HK$ 10000 while HK$ M2 will decrease by HK$50000 
C. HK$ M2 will decrease by HK$ 50000 while HK$ M3 will remain unchangedD. HK$ M1 will remain unchanged while HK$ M2 will decrease by HK$40000 
陳先生從他在香港一家銀行的儲蓄戶口提取港元$50000，將其中$40000港元經銀行匯往英國，其餘的$10000港元換成新發行的$10港元，並放在他在銀行開設的保險箱。下列哪一項是上述行動對港元貨幣供應的即時影響？
A. 港元貨幣供應定義二和定義三都會減少40000港元。B. 港元貨幣供應定義一會增加10000港元，而港元貨幣供應定義二都會減少50000港元。
C. 港元貨幣供應定義二會減少50000港元，而港元貨幣供應定義三會維持不變。D. 港元貨幣供應定義一會維持不變，而港元貨幣供應定義二會減少40000港元。
Example Question 16 CE2004MCQ37
Which of the following would decrease the money supply M2 in Hong Kong?
A. Mr. Au repays a HK$10 000 loan in cash to a licensed bank in Hong Kong.
B. Mr. Bean withdraws HK$20 000 from a savings account with a licensed bank in Hong Kong and holds it as cash.
C. Mr. Chan transfers HK$30 000 from his savings account to his current account with a licensed bank in Hong Kong.
D.

rent account with a licensed bank in Hong Kong.

下列哪一項會減少香港貨幣供應定義二 (M2)？
A. 區先生以現金償還港幣10 000元的債項予香港某持牌銀行。
B. 邊先生從香港某持牌銀行的儲蓄戶口提取港幣20 000元，並以現金方式持有。
C. 陳先生從香港某持牌銀行的儲蓄戶口轉帳港幣30 000元至同一銀行的支票戶口。
D. 丁先生從香港某有限制牌照銀行的定期存款戶口提取港幣40 000元，並以全數匯往外國。
Example Question 17 CE2005MCQ38
Suppose Mrs. Wong withdraws HK$300 000 from her saving deposit with a commercial bank. She converts HK$200 000 of the above amount into Renminbi and deposits it into a Renminbi saving deposit with the same bank. She keeps the remaining HK$100 000 at home. The immediate effect of the above event is:
A. Hong Kong dollar money supply M1 would increase by $100 000
B. Hong Kong dollar money supply M2 would decrease by $300 000
C. Hong Kong dollar money supply M2 would remain changed
D. Hong Kong dollar money supply M3 would increase by $100 000
假設黃太從一家商業銀行的儲蓄戶口提取港幣$300 000，將其中港幣$200 000轉換為人民幣，並存於同一銀行的人民幣儲蓄戶口內，餘下港幣$100 000則留在家中。上

metadata.source: Ch18 Money Supply and Money Creation PP QB (CHECKED) 2.0.docx

chunks.text:
200 000轉換為人民幣，並存於同一銀行的人民幣儲蓄戶口內，餘下港幣$100 000則留在家中。上述事件的即時變化是
A. 港元貨幣供應定義一(M1)會增加$100 000。B. 港元貨幣供應定義二(M2)會減少$300 000。
C. 港元貨幣供應定義二(M2)會維持不變。D. 港元貨幣供應定義三(M3)會增加$100 000。
Example Question 18 CE2006MCQ35
Mr. Chan withdraws HK$ 20 000 from his deposit in a restricted licence bank in HK. He later puts HK$15 000 as time deposit in a licensed bank in Hong Kong and holds the remaining HK$5 000 as cash. The immediate effect of the above action on the Hong Kong dollar money supply would be:
A. Both M2 and M3 decreaseB. Both M1 and M2 remain unchanged
C. M1 increases while M2 remains unchangedD. M1 increases while M3 remains unchanged
陳先生從本港某有限制牌照銀行的存款中提取20 000港元，然後將其中15 000港元存放於本港某持牌銀行中作為定期存款，其餘5 000港元則持作現金。上述行動對港元貨幣供應的即時影響會是：
A. 貨幣供應M2及M3皆減少。B. 貨幣供應M1及M2皆維持不變。
C. 貨幣供應M1增加而貨幣供應M2維持不變。D. 貨幣供應M1增加而貨幣供應M3維持不變。

mand deposits in a licensed bank. What will be the immediate effect of the above actions on the money supply of Hong Kong?

Marking Scheme:
(a)Excess reserves = $300 million - $1000 million x 20% = $100 million(1)
(b)Old money supply = $1000 million + $200 million
= $1200 million
New money supply = ($300 million + $200 million) x 1/20% + $0 million
= $2500 million
Maximum possible change in the money supply = $2500 million - $1200 million
= $1300 million (4)
(a)超額儲備= 300百萬元 - 1000百萬元x 20% = 100百萬元(1)
(b)舊的貨幣供應= 1000百萬元 + 200百萬元
= 1200百萬元
新的貨幣供應= (300百萬元 + 200百萬元) x 1 / 20% + 0百萬元
= 2500百萬元
貨幣供應的最大可能轉變= 2500百萬元 - 1200百萬元
= 1300百萬元(4)

chunks.text:
____________________________________________
Marking Scheme:
(a)Required reserve ratio = (600-300)/1500 = 20%(1)
(b)New required ratio = 20 – 5 = 15 (%)
New deposit = 1500 + (600 – 0.15x1500)/0.15 = 4000 ($million)
New money supply= 4000 + 100 = 4100 ($million)
In real-world practice of monetary policy, adjustment in the required reserve ratio (RRR) is understood as a change in percentage point or basis point (i.e. a level change in RRR) rather than a percentage in RRR. The following answers are also accepted in case the candidate (mis)understood the 5% reduction in RRR as a percentage change rather than change in percentage point. New required reserve ratio = 20% x (1 - 5%) = 19%, New deposit = $3157.89 million , New money supply = $3257.89 million(3)
(a)法定儲備比率 = (600-300)/1500 = 20%(1)
(b)新法定儲備比率 = 20 – 5 = 15 (%)
新存款額 = 1500 + (600 – 0.15x1500)/0.15 = 4000（百萬元）
新貨幣供應 = 4000 + 100 = 4100（百萬元）
真實世界中的貨幣政策調整法定儲備比率(RRR)時應理解為「百分點」或「基數」的改變（即RRR水平的改變）
而非RRR作某百分比的改變。當考生（誤）將RRR下降5%當成百分比的改變而不是百分點的改變，下列答案屬
可接受。新法定儲備比率

chunks.text:
Learning Focus:
Learning Focus:
Chapter 18 _ Money Supply & Deposit Creation
Type 1 (Money Supply)
Example Question 1 CE1990MCQ32
If Mr. Wong transfers HK$100 from his savings account to his current account,
A. the total amount of bank deposits will increase.B. the total money supply as measured by M1 will decrease.
C. the total money supply as measured by M2 will not change.D. the total money supply as measured by M3 will increase.
若黃先生將港幣$100從他的儲蓄戶口轉存入往來戶口，則
A. 銀行存款總額會增加。B. 貨幣供應定義一M1會減少。
C. 貨幣供應定義二M2不會改變。D. 貨幣供應定義三M3會增加。
Example Question 2 CE1990MCQ45
Which of the following will increase the money supply in an economy?
A. A citizen withdraws cash from a bank and keeps it in a safe.B. A manufacturer repays a bank loan.
C. A bank grants a loan to a real estate developer.D. A bank increases the amount of excess reserves it holds.
下列哪一種情況會增加貨幣供應？
A. 一市民從銀行提取現金後，將它存藏於夾萬內。B. 一廠家償還它的銀行貸款。
C. 一銀行提供貸款予一地產發展商。D. 一銀行增加它所持有超額儲備的數量。
Example Question 3 CE1991MCQ34
Which of the following is a component of Hong Kong’s mone

A. savings deposits with licensed banks
B. time deposits with deposit-taking companies
C. negotiable certificates of deposit held by banks
D. one-thousand-dollar gold coins issued by the Hong Kong government and held by banks
以下哪一項是香港貨幣供應定義二M2的組成部分？
A. 存於持牌銀行的儲蓄存款B. 存於接受存款公司的定期存款
C. 銀行所持有的可轉讓存款證D. 銀行所持有的由港府發行的千元面額金幣
Example Question 4 CE1992MCQ32
In Hong Kong, savings deposits with licensed banks are 
A. legal tenderB. included in the money supply M1
C. included in the money supply M2D. included in the money supply M2, but excluded from the money supply M3
在香港，存於持牌銀行的儲蓄戶口存款是
A. 法定貨幣B. 包括在貨幣供應定義一(M1)內。
C. 包括在貨幣供應定義二(M2)內。D. 包括在貨幣供應定義二(M2)內，但不包括在貨幣供應定義三(M3)內。
Example Question 5 CE1994MCQ43
If an individual receives a cheque for $1000 from his friend and deposits it in his savings account, Hong Kong’s money supply M1 will _______ and M2 will ________.
A. increase; increase B. remain unchanged; increase
C. decrease; remain unchanged D. remain unchange

e

A. 上升；上升B. 不變；上升
C. 下降；不變      D. 不變；不變
Example Question 6 CE1995MCQ43
Mr. Chan withdraws $600 000 from his savings deposit with a commercial bank and then deposits $500 000 with a restricted licence bank. The remaining $100 000 is kept at home. The immediate effect is that
A. M1 will increase by $100 000 B. M2 will decrease by $600 000
C. M2 will not change D. M3 will increase by $500 000
陳先生從一商業銀行的儲蓄戶口提取$600 000，然後將$500 000 存於一有限制牌照銀行的戶口內，餘下的$100 000則留在家中。上述事件的即時變化是
A. 貨幣供應定義一(M1)會增加$100 000B. 貨幣供應定義二(M2)會減少$600 000
C. 貨幣供應定義二(M2)維持不變D. 貨幣供應定義三(M3)會增加$500 000

(1) M1 decrease by $200000
(2) M2 remains unchanged
(3) M3 increase by $200000

(1) 貨幣供應定義一會減少20萬元。
(2) 貨幣供應定義二維持不變。
(3) 貨幣供應定義三增加20萬元。

C. a new deposit of HK$5000 in a savings account with a bank in Hong Kong

D. 香港某銀行一位客戶的往來存款戶口被提取港幣$5000現金。

以下哪一項導致本港貨幣供應定義一即時有所改變？
A. 你將父親給予你的$500現金存入往來戶口內。
B. 你支付屋主$4000現金，他存入夾萬中。
C. 一場大火燒毀一間銀行內大部分紙幣。
D. 你用支票買入價值$10 000的傢俬，店主將支票存入他的定期存款戶口內。
Example Question 11 CE1999MCQ39
Suppose in a bank in Hong Kong, Mary withdraws HK$3 000 from her savings account and changes it for US dollars in cash. Which of the following statements about the HKD money supply is correct?
A. HK$ M1 will increase while HK$ M2 will remain unchanged.
B. HK$ M1 will remain unchanged while HK$ M3 will decrease.
C. Both HK$ M1 and HK$ M3 will remain unchanged.
D. Both HK$ M1 and HK$ M2 will decrease.
假設在香港某銀行裡，紅玉從儲蓄戶口中提取港幣3000元，並用它換取了美元現金。下列哪一項關於港元貨幣供應的描述是正確的？
A. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）會上升，而港元貨幣供應（定義二）會維持不變。
B. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）會維持不變，而港元貨幣供應（定義三）會下跌。
C. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）及（定義三）均維持不變。
D. 港元貨幣供應（定義一）及（定義二）均會下跌。
Example Question 12 CE2000MCQ27
Mr. Wong withdraws $500 000 in cash from a deposit-taking company. He then deposits $300 000 with a bank as ti

chunks.text:
mpany. He then deposits $300 000 with a bank as time deposit and remits the remaining $200 000 overseas for the settlement of debts.
Which of the following about the money supply M1 and M3 is correct?

Marking Scheme:
Credit creation process 
When the central bank purchases bonds from commercial banks, more cash is injected to the commercial banks as reserve and there would be excess reserves in the banking system.(1)
The banks would lend out the excess reserves.(1)
And the bank loans will be re-deposited into the banking system.(1)
The process will go on and on (until the actual reserve are equal to required reserves)
No. The monetary base will remain unchanged as the sum of cash in public circulation and reserves in the banking system will not be affected by the credit creation process.(2)
存款創造過程:
當中央銀行向商業銀行購入債券，更多的現金會注入商業銀行成為儲備，而銀行系統會有超額儲備。(1)
銀行會借出其超額儲備。(1)
銀行貸款會再存入銀行系統。(1)
該過程會繼續（直到實際儲備等於法定儲備）。(1)
否。貨幣基礎會維持不變，因為在公眾流通的現金和銀行系統內的儲備的總和不會受存款創造的過程影響。(2)

真實世界中的貨幣政策調整法定儲備比率(RRR)時應理解為「百分點」或「基數」的改變（即RRR水平的改變）
而非RRR作某百分比的改變。當考生（誤）將RRR下降5%當成百分比的改變而不是百分點的改變，下列答案屬
可接受。新法定儲備比率 = 20% x (1 - 5%) = 19%, 新存款額 = 3157.89（百萬元）, 新貨幣供應 = 3257.89（百萬
元）(3)

nterest rate,

 Students are expected to

(ii)  both V and Y to be constant.

Intentional change in money supply and / or interest rate by the central bank or the government to achieve certain economic goals.

Fiscal policy is used to achieve the same economics goal with monetary policy by changing the government revenue / government expenditure.
財政政策是通過改變政府的財政收入和支出來實現與貨幣政策相同的政策 / 目標

Both Monetary Base and Money Supply are only calculated the amount in the circulation not the total amount issued. (Legal tender hold by central bank is not included in monetary base.)
貨幣基礎和貨幣供應都只計算流通中的數量而不是發行的總量。(中央銀行持有的貨幣並不包括在貨幣基礎內。)

20.4 Monetary Base and Money Supply Concept Corner: 
1.Monetary base = M0 T / F
貨幣基礎 = M0
∵ 
2.Money supply = Cash held by public + Cash reserve T / F
貨幣供應 = 公眾持有的現金 + 現金儲備
∵ 
3.Deposit = Reserve + Loan T / F
存款 = 儲備 + 貸款
∵ 
4.Money in safety box is counted as cash in the circulation T / F
在保險箱中的現金計算在流通中的貨幣中。
∵ 
5.Total issue of legal tender = Cash reserve + Currency in the circulation T / F
總發行的法定貨幣 = 現金儲備 + 流通中的貨幣
∵ 
6.When monetary base increase, money supply must increase T / F
當貨幣基礎上升，貨幣供應必定上升。
∵

20.6 Expansionary / Contractionary Monetary Policy Concept Corner: 
 擴張性 / 緊縮性貨幣政策概念釐清：
1.Increase in money supply is an expansionary monetary policy T / F
貨幣供應上升是一種擴張性貨幣政策
∵ 
2.Increase in RRR leads to a smaller money supply T / F
法定儲備比率上升將導致貨幣供應下跌。
∵ 
3.When the central bank open market purchase of bonds, price level will decrease in short runT / F
當中央銀行在公開市場購買債券，物價水平將在短期下跌。
∵ 
4.Increase in discount rate will lead to a lower interest rate T / F
貼現率上升將導致利率下跌。 
∵ 
5.Increase in interest rate will lead to a higher discount rate T / F
利率上升將導致較高的貼現率。
∵ 
6.Drop in money supply is a contractionary monetary policy T / F
減少貨幣供應是一種緊縮性貨幣政策。
∵ 
7.Increase in RRR is an example of a contractionary monetary policy T / F
法定儲備比率上升是緊縮性貨幣政策的例子之一。
∵ 
8.Issue currency will lead to an increase in price level in long run T / F
發行貨幣將導致物價水平在長期上升。
∵ 
9.When the public purchase bonds from the central bank, both money supply and monetary

metadata.source: Ch20 Monetary Policy and QTM NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
the central bank, both money supply and monetary base drop T / F
當公眾從中央銀行購買債券，貨幣供應及貨幣基礎將會同時下跌。
∵ 
10.Drop in RRR will lead to a larger monetary base T / F
法定儲備比率下跌時，將導致貨幣基礎上升。
∵ 
ANS: TTFFF TTTTF

MV≡PY

Average number of time a unit of money changes hands in a specific period of time 
E.g. V = 4 implies  
Money stock turns over 4 times in a year for financial transaction 
Money stock is kept on average for 3 months (12months / 4 times) before spent  
特定期間內單位貨幣易手的平均次數

   消費物價指數

20.7 Equation of Exchange Concept Corner: 
1.Equation of exchange is an identity. 
      交易方程式是一條恆等式。                          T / F

T / F

∵

V = 特定期間內單位貨幣易手的平均次數                                          T / F
∵

PY = 實質產出 T / F
∵

Y = 名義產出       T / F
∵ 
8.Equation of exchange is always true
交易方程式永遠是正確的。            T / F
∵

20.8Quantity Theory of Money (QTM) 貨幣數量論
Quantity Theory of Money 貨幣數量論 
Predict how a change in money supply affect general price

metadata.source: Ch20 Monetary Policy and QTM NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
how a change in money supply affect general price level 預測貨幣供應的變動如何影響一般物價水平
Assumption 假設:
1.V is assumed to be constant   V假設為常數
-Velocity affected by habit, institutional and technological factors 
(e.g. payment system, consumption pattern)
流通受習慣、制度和技術因素影響
（例如：支付系統、消費模式）
assume to be constant 假設為常數
2.Y 
is Variable in Short Run 短期內是可變的
is Constant in Long Run (Classical QTM) 長期不變（古典貨幣數量論）
Examples in both short run and long run: 短期和長期的例子：
        
***Reminder 提醒:
In Short Run,↑in M does NOT necessarily implies↑in P or Y. 
In Long Run,↑in M MUST implies↑in P, vice versa.
在短期，M中的↑不一定意味著 P或 Y中的↑。
在長期，M中的↑必須意味著 P 中的↑，反之亦然。
20.8 Quantity Theory of Money (QTM) Concept Corner貨幣數量論 (QTM)
1.Quantity Theory of Money predicts how money supply is affected by price level 
貨幣數量論預測貨幣供應如何受物價水平影響T / F
∵ 
2. V is assumed to be constant in QTM
貨幣數量論中，假設V為常數。          T / F
∵ 
3.Y is assumed to be constant in QTM during long run
在長期，假設Y維持不變。          T / F
∵ 
4.Classical QTM implies short run
古典貨幣數量論意味著短期。           T / F
∵

metadata.source: Ch20 Monetary Policy and QTM NOTE (CHECKED).docx

chunks.text:
mplies short run
古典貨幣數量論意味著短期。           T / F
∵ 
5.Adopting classical QTM, increase in M leads to increase in P
採用古典貨幣數量論，M的增加導致P的增加。                  T / F
∵ 
6.Adopting QTM, increase in M must lead to increase in PY
採用貨幣數量論，M的增加必然導致PY的增加。         T / F
∵ 
7.Adopting QTM, increase in M may lead to a larger increase in P
採用貨幣數量論，M的增加可能會導致P更大的增加。           T / F
∵ 
8.% increase in M is equal to % increase in P during long run when adopting QTM
在長期，採用貨幣數量論時，M 的百分比增加等於 P 的百分比增加。   T / F
∵ 
9.% increase in M may lead to a larger % increase in PY when adopting QTM in short run
在短期，採用貨幣數量論時，M 的百分比增加可能會導致 PY 的百分比增加更大。      T / F
∵ 
10.% increase in M may lead to a smaller % increase in P when adopting QTM in short run 
在短期，採用貨幣數量論時，M 的百分比增加可能會導致 P 的百分比增加較小。      T / F
∵

20.8Quantity Theory of Money (QTM) (cont) 貨幣數量論
Short Run 短期
Case 1: Money Supply increase 5%

(3) 實質產出水平會較高

(a)收縮性財政政策的副作用：
對產出的負面影響（本地生產總值下降）：因為政府支出下降令總需求下降
對產出的負面影響（本地生產總值下降）：因為銷售稅/入息稅增加令消費/投資下降，總需求下降
對勞工的負面影響（抑制作用）並對就業（及產出）的負面影響：又增加入息稅所引致

增加貨幣供應會令利率下降，並增加投資，在短期零實質產出上升。已知該經濟有限知的資源，有更多的勞工（及/或資本）會用作生產更多產出。這令就業上升，失業下降（而收縮性財政政策不會導致產出和就業上升）。
缺點：
在長期（總供應曲線是垂直），增加貨幣供應不會影響產出和就業。但如果財政赤字增長太快，令貨幣供應增長率高於實際產生的增長率，高通脹會出現（而收縮性財政政策不會導致通脹上升）。

nvest more, implying an increase in aggregate demand. As a result, aggregate output (Y) would rise in the short run, but long-run output (Yf) would remain unchanged, so that the deflationary gap (= Y – Yf) would become narrower. (4)

22.5Exchange Rate – Hong Kong’s Linked Exchange Rate System
22.5  匯率 – 香港的聯繫匯率制度
Hong Kong: Linked Exchange Rate System
maintained by currency board system
(HKD must be back up by USD according to the linked exchange rate of HKD) 
Purpose: Stabilize the exchange rate of HKD in terms of USD (1 USD : 7.8 HKD)
香港：聯繫匯率制度
由貨幣發行局制度維持穩定
(港幣必須按照聯繫匯率底下同等價值的美元作為支持) 
目的：穩定港元兌美元的匯率
1美元：  7.8港元
Fixed Exchange Rate ()
Issuing banknotes
Note-issuing banks buy Certificates of Indebtedness (CI) with US dollar from Exchange fund 
At a fixed exchange rate of 1 USD to 7.8 HKD
Issuing HKD banknotes
Banks may transact HKD & USD with Exchange Fund at the fixed rate
固定匯率 ()
發行紙幣
發鈔銀行用美元從外匯基金購買負債證明書 (CI) 
在1美元兌7.8港元的固定匯率
發行港元紙幣
銀行可按固定匯率用外匯基金買賣港元及美元
Flexible Exchange Rate ()
In foreign exchange market
The exchange rate between USD and the HKD is to be determined by Market Force 
Non-b

the HKD is to be determined by Market Force 
Non-bank public can buy or sell USD and HKD to earn the capital gain
Buy USD when USD 1 : HKD 7
Sell USD when USD 1 : HKD 8
But is stabilized around the linked rate.
浮動/彈性匯率()
在外匯市場
美元兌港元匯率由市場力量決定
非銀行公眾可以買賣美元和港元以賺取資本收益
當1美元：7港元時買入美元
當1美元：8港元時賣走美元

22.7Balance of Payment Account 國際收支平衡表
Balance of Payment Account
Statement that summarizes the economic transaction of an economy 
In a specific period (1 quarter / 1 year)
Transaction involve Receipt / Inflow 
Credit (+) 
Transaction involve Payme

pt / Inflow 
Credit (+) 
Transaction involve Payment / Outflow 
Debit (-)
國際收支平衡表
總結經濟體的經濟交易
在特定期間（1季度 / 1年）
交易涉及收款 / 流入 
 貸方    (+)
交易涉及支付 / 流出 / 外流
 借方    (-)
Balance of payment account involve 2 separate accounts
Current Account
Capital and Financial Account
Current Account worsen 
Balance of Payment tends to be worsen (not a must)
Capital and Financial Account worsen 
Balance of Payment tends to be worsen (not a must)
When both of the accounts are worsen 
Balance of Payment will be worsen, vice versa…
國際收支平衡表涉及兩個獨立帳戶
往來帳戶
資本與金融帳戶
經常帳惡化 
國際收支趨於惡化（非必然）
資本與金融帳惡化 
國際收支趨於惡化（非必然）
當兩個帳戶都惡化時 
國際收支將會惡化。反之亦然…
Traps:
If Inflow > Outflow,  Surplus
If Inflow < Outflow,  Deficit 
↑in inflow ≠ Surplus 
↓in inflow ≠ Deficit 
↑in outflow ≠ Deficit 
↓in outflow ≠ Surplus 
陷阱：
若流入 > 外流， 盈餘
若流入 < 外流， 赤字
流入 ↑ ≠ 盈餘
流入 ↓ ≠ 赤字
流出 ↑ ≠ 赤字
流出 ↓ ≠ 盈餘
22.7Balance of Payment Account (cont) 國際收支平衡表（續）
The Current Account 經常帳
Goods
Also called “Visible trade account”
Visible trade balance = Domestic Export of goods + Re-export of goods–

e = Domestic Export of goods + Re-export of goods–Import of goods
E.g. Cars, Wines, Shoes, Iphones, Books
貨物
也稱為“有形貿易帳”
有形貿易差額 = 國內出口貨物 + 貨物轉口 - 進口貨物
例如 汽車、葡萄酒、鞋子、書籍
Services
Also called “Invisible trade account”
Invisible trade balance = Export of services–Import of services
E.g. Communications, Financial services, Transportation, Business services, License fee, Brokerage fee 
***Goods bought by the tourist is included in invisible account instead of visible account
ii) 服務
也稱為“無形貿易帳”
無形貿易差額 = 服務出口 - 服務進口
例如 通訊、金融服務、交通、商業服務、許可證費、經紀費
***遊客購買的商品不計入有形貿易帳，而是計入無形貿易帳
Factor Income
External transactions of factor services
Net income from abroad = Income received from abroad – Income paid to abroad 
E.g. Compensation, Investment, Wages, Rent, Dividend
iii) 要素收入
要素服務的對外交易
海外要素收入淨流入 = 海外要素收入流入 – 海外要素收入流出
例如 工資、投資、薪資、租金、股息
Current Transfer
Unilateral transfer without economics value
Net current transfer = Transfer from abroad – Transfers to abroad 
E.g. Remittances, Donations, Pensions, Gifts
iv) 經常轉移
單方面轉讓，無經濟價值
經常轉移淨額

tions, Pensions, Gifts
iv) 經常轉移
單方面轉讓，無經濟價值
經常轉移淨額 = 經常轉移流入 –經常轉移流出
例如 匯款、捐贈、退休金、禮品
Current account balance
經常帳餘額
=
Visible trade balance + Invisible trade balance + 
Net income from abroad + Net current transfers
有形貿易差額 + 無形貿易差額 +
海外要素收入淨流入 + 經常轉移淨額
22.7Balance of Payment Account (cont) 國際收支平衡表（續）
The Capital and Financial Account 資本與金融帳戶
External inflows & outflows of Assets / Liabilities
E.g. Setting up a factory, Buying shares in the stock market, Equipment, Land, Property, Patents, Copyright, Exchange Fund Bills, Deposits
資產 / 負債的對外流入與流出
例如 設立工廠、買入股票、設備、土地、物業、專利、版權、外匯基金票據、存款
Reminders 提醒:
Investment vs Return of investment 投資與投資報酬
1. Sales of stock 股票出售 
1. the return of dividend 股息回報
2. Sales of a property 房產銷售
2. the return of rental income租金收入回報
3. Purchase of bond 購買債券
3. the return of interest利息回報
Case 1:
Increase in inflow of Capital and Finance Account
Capital and Financial Account may not be surplus
Since the original condition may be a large deficit… 
情況一：
即使資本及金融帳流入增加
資本及金融帳亦不一定有盈餘
因為原本可能已經有巨額的

cit… 
情況一：
即使資本及金融帳流入增加
資本及金融帳亦不一定有盈餘
因為原本可能已經有巨額的赤字…

22.8  More about the Balance of Payment Account (cont) 更多有關國際收支平衡表（續）
Balance of Payment 國際收支平衡表
Market Balance of Payment 巿場國際收支平衡表
Accounting Balance of Payment 會計國際收支平衡表
Market Balance of Payment
Equal to the sum of all market transaction 
Current Account Balance + Capital and Financial Account - Increase of Reserve Asset + Outflow of Reserve Asset (Inflow of Reserve Asset - Outflow of Reserve Asset) = Net Change in Reserve Asset
Current Account Balance + Capital and Financial Account - Net Change in Reserve Asset

19.5 Calculation of Income Earning Asset Concept Corner: 
1.Nominal interest rate = Inflation rate + Real interest rate T / F
名義利率 = 通脹率 + 實質利率
∵ 
2.Actual real interest rate = Realized real interest rate T / F
實際實質利率 = 實際實質利率
∵ 
3.Nominal return / nominal interest rate of holding cash can be both positive and negative T / F
持有現金的名義回報 / 名義利率可以是正數或負數。
∵ 
4.Actual real interest rate of holding cash = - actual inflation rate T / F
持有現金實際實質利率 = - 實際通

= - actual inflation rate T / F
持有現金實際實質利率 = - 實際通脹率
∵ 
5.Opportunity cost of holding money = - nominal interest rate T / F
持有現金的機會成本 = - 名義利率
∵ 
6.Opportunity cost of holding money is forgoing the nominal return of income earning assetT / F
持有現金的機會成本是放棄持有創收資產的名義回報。
∵ 
7.Stock, property and debentures are a kind of income earning assetT / F
股票、物業及債券均屬於創收資產。
∵ 
8.Actual real interest rate must be larger than expected real interest rate T / F
實際實質利率必定大於預期實質利率。
∵ 
9.If actual inflation rate is higher than expected inflation rate, actual real interest rate will be lower than expected when holding cash T / F
當持有現金時，若實際通脹率高於預期通脹率，實際實質利率將會比預期低。
∵ 
10.For income earning asset, actual real interest rate = - actual inflation rate T / F
問及創收資產，實際實質利率 = - 實際通脹率
∵ 
ANS: TTFTF TTFTF

19.10Money Supply Curve 貨幣供應曲線
Money supply is determined by central bank
When the central bank of an economy (or HKMA) chooses to use the money supply as a policy instrument of monetary policy, the amount of money supply will be independent of nominal interest rate. Money supply is not determined by the interest rate, so money supply cure is vertical.
Vertical Money Supply Curve (since Money Supply is not affected by nominal interest value)
貨幣供應量由中央銀行決定。
當一個經濟體的中央銀行（或香港金管局）選擇使用貨幣供應作為貨幣政策的工具時，貨幣供應量的數目將會獨立於名義利率。貨幣供應量不受利率影響，因此貨幣供應曲線時垂直的。
貨幣供應曲線是垂直的（因為貨幣供應量不受名義利率的影響）

International Trade and Finance

 Important of trade to Hong Kong’s economy 
Trade barriers
 Types

 Trade barriers faced by Hong Kong
Hong Kong’s attempts to overcome trade barriers 
 Trade promotion 
 Role of the HKSAR Government 
 Role of international economic institutions (e.g. World Trade Organisation) 
 Students are expected to 
(i)  understand the meaning of absolute advantage and comparative advantage, 
(ii)  explain which country has an absolute advantage in the production of a good / goods in a given case, 
(iii)  explain which country has a comparative advantage in the production of a good in a given case,(iv)  understand the condit

a good in a given case,(iv)  understand the condition(s) in which mutually beneficial trade is possible;(v)  calculate the gains from trade, and 
(vi)  state the principle of comparative advantage. 
 Students are expected to illustrate the importance of trade to Hong Kong with reference to given data.
 Students are expected to understand different types of trade barriers including tariff, quota, import surcharge, and embargo, etc.
 Students are expected to analyse the effects of tariff and quota on price, domestic output, import and consumption quantity, tariff revenue (if applicable), consumer surplus, producer surplus, total social surplus, and deadweight loss for a small open economy.
 Starting from S4 in 2013/14, i.e.2016 HKDSE Examination, students are NOT expected to analyse the effects of tariff and quota for a small open economy on consumer surplus, producer surplus, total social surplus and deadweight loss.

1.Principle of Comparative Advantage
The principle of comparative advantage states that if each country specialises in producing the good in which it has a comparative advantage, the total world output will increase.

21.3Absolute Advantage (Determine which country is more productive)
絕對優勢（決定哪個國家更具生產力）
A country has absolute advantage in producing a good if
A country can produce a larger amount of good than other country with the same amount of resources
A country can produce the larger amount of good by using fewer resources than other country
A country can produce the same amount of good by using fewer resources than other country
一個國家生產某一物品享有絕對優勢，如果：
相比其他國家，該國用同一數量的資源可以生產更多數量的該物品。
相比其他國家，該國用較少數量的資源可以生產更多數量的該物品。
相比其他國家，該國用較少數量的資源可以生產同一數量的該物品。

China’s opportunity cost in producing 1 unit of Book = 4 ÷ 2 = 2 units of Paper
USA’s opportunity cost in producing 1 unit of Book = 1 ÷ 1 = 1 unit of Paper
USA has comparative advantage in producing Book since it has a lower opportunity cost in producing Book. 
Therefore, USA will export Book and China will import Book.

Concept Corner: 
1.Enjoying a comparative advantage implies the country can produce more goods    享有比較優勢意味著該國可以生產更多物品                          T / F
∵ 
2.Enjoying a comparative advantage implies the country can produce different kinds of good at a lower cost 
    享有比較優勢意味著該國可以以較低成本生產不同物品T / F
∵ 
3.Enjoying a comparative advantage implies the country can produce the same amount of good with a lower cost 
    享有比較優勢意味著該國可以以較低成本生產同一數量的物品T / F
∵ 
4.There is no relationship between absolute advantage and comparative advantage    絕對優勢與比較優勢之間沒有正比或反比的關係                        T / F
∵ 
5.Enjoying comparative advantage implies the country will most likely export the good if there is any potential gain.
    享有比較優勢意味著該國很有可能會出口該物品，如果有任何潛在得益      T / F
∵ 
6.A comparative advantage will exist with a comparative disadvantage
    一個比較優勢會與比較劣勢同時存在                     T / F
∵ 
7.A country may have both comparative advantage and comparative disadvantage on the same good    一個國家可能同時在同一物品產

ve disadvantage on the same good    一個國家可能同時在同一物品產具有比較優勢和比較劣勢  T / F
∵ 
8.Enjoying an absolute advantage will most likely enjoying comparative advantage    享有絕對優勢的同時很有可能享有比較優勢                          T / F
∵ 
ANS: FFTTT TFF

Effect on Mutually Beneficial Terms of Trade
Without Transportation Cost:
Mutually Beneficial terms of Trade: 2 unit of Paper < 1 unit of Book < 4 unit of Paper 
If each country has to pay 0.1P as transportation cost,
China will gain if the Export Price > China’s opportunity cost + Transportation Cost 
(i.e. Export price > 2.1P)
USA will gain if the Import Price + Transportation Cost < USA’s opportunity cost 
(i.e. Import price < 3.9P)
New Mutually Beneficial Terms of Trad

rice < 3.9P)
New Mutually Beneficial Terms of Trade = 2.1 unit of Paper < 1 unit of Book < 3.9 unit of Paper
If each country has to pay 0.2P as transportation cost,
China will gain if the Export Price > China’s opportunity cost + Transportation Cost 
USA will gain if the Import Price + Transportation Cost < USA’s opportunity cost 
New Mutually Beneficial Terms of Trade = 2.1 unit of Paper < 1 unit of Book < 3.9 unit of Paper
*****Reminder:
If the transportation cost > total gain from trade (i.e. 4P – 2P = 2P), trade will not exist. 
Since it is not mutually beneficial.

21.8Reasons for International Trade國際貿易的原因
Reasons… 
Unable to be self-sufficient
Most economies are not able to produce all kinds good, therefore, they need to trade and import the necessities they need for subsistence.
Economies of scale
International specialization leads to an increase in volume of production which brings economies of scale and the production cost can be reduced
More choices in goods and services
After trade, different countries may enjoy distinctive product all around the globe.
Improvement in technology
Importing capital and technology from some developed countries.
Suppression of domestic monopoly
Local market may initially be under a monopoly. After trade with foreign countries, they may import some substitutes for the local good under monopoly. This helps decrease the domestic price and stimulate the product development.
Other benefits
Enhance exchange of culture and knowledge, reduces misunderstanding.

1. 無法自給自足
大多數經濟體無法生產所有種類的物品，因此，它們需要貿易和進口維持生計所需的必需品。
2. 規模經濟
國際專業化導致產量增加，帶來規模經濟，生產成本得以降低
3. 更多的物品和服務選擇
通過貿易，不同的國家可以在全球範圍內享受到各具特色的產品。
4. 技術改進
從一些發達國家引進資金和技術。
5. 打壓本地壟斷
本地市場最初可能處於壟斷狀態。 與外國進行貿易後，他們可能會在壟斷下進口一些當地物品的替代品。 這有助於降低本地價格並刺激產品開發。
6. 其他好處
加強文化和知識的交流，減少誤解。
